fields.title: Read the passage below and answer the questions that follow:
fields.content:
In a small village situated between rolling hills, there lived a boy named Morrison. His family had been farmers for generations, waking before sunrise to tend to the fields. Life was simple but difficult and education was often seen as a luxury rather than a necessity. Morrison, however, was different. He had an insatiable curiosity, he wanted to understand the stars that lit up the night sky, the rivers that carved through the land and the stories hidden within the pages of old books.
One day, a traveling teacher visited the village, offering lessons to those who wished to learn. Morrison's heart raced with excitement, but his father hesitated. "Books won't plough the fields," he said. "Knowledge won't put food on the table."
But Morrison was determined. He woke earlier than ever, finishing his farm work before the sun climbed too high and then ran to the lessons. With each passing day, his mind expanded. He learned about science, history and languages, things he had never imagined.
Years later, his education opened doors his ancestors never knew existed. He became an engineer, designing irrigation systems that transformed his village's farmlands. The once-dry fields flourished and the harvests grew plentiful. His people no longer struggled to survive; they thrived.
One evening, as Morrison stood beneath the same starlit sky that had once filled him with questions, his father placed a hand on his shoulder and smiled. "I was wrong," he admitted. "Books may not plough
the fields, but they can teach us how to make them richer than ever before.»

fields.title: Hapa kuna nakala ya hadithi hiyo, maswali yake, na majibu sahihi kulingana na mtaala wa CBC Grade 9
fields.content:
Hapo zamani za kale paliondokea Nyoka na Jongoo. Nyoka alikuwa hana macho. Naye Jongoo alikuwa na macho na miguu. Nyoka na Jongoo walikuwa marafiki waliopendana kama chanda na pete. Marafiki hawa wawili walifanya mambo mengi pamoja. Zaidi, walifaana kwa jua na kwa mvua.

Siku moja Nyoka alimwambia Jongoo, "Ndugu yangu, ninasikia ukisifika kwa utanashati. Wanasema weusi wako ni kama wa mpingo. Tena mwili wako umenyooka kama yule aliyejipaka mafuta ya uto. Miguu yako ninaambiwa ni mingi ajabu. Haihesabiki. Ninatamani kuona hayo yote. Maskini mimi sina macho. Nitakuonaje? Hiyo miguu yako mingi kama mchanga wa baharini ningepata lau moja. Ninaomba uniazime jicho lako moja nikuone." "Pole ndugu yangu. Hivyo ndivyo maumbile yalivyo. Unapata hiki unakosa kile. Nitakupa macho yangu unitazame upendavyo. Bora tu unirudishie baada ya saa moja. Siwezi kukuazima jicho moja. Macho hufanya kazi vizuri yakiwa mawili." Alisema Jongoo na kumwazima Nyoka macho.

Nyoka aliyapokea macho ya Jongoo kwa moyo mkunjufu. Aliyaajabika maumbile ya mazingira yake na ya rafiki yake jongoo. Aliweza pia kujitazama na kuona alivyo.

"Lo! Kumbe ulimwengu ni wa kweli. Unapendeza hivi? Afadhali ningekosa kila kitu lakini nipewe macho." Nyoka alisema huku akiangalia huku na kule.

Muda wa kuazimwa macho ulikuwa umezidi kupungua. Jongoo alimwambia Nyoka amrudishie macho yake. Nyoka alikataa kurudisha macho. Akasema, "Sikupi macho yako. Tosheka na wingi wa miguu yako. Tamaa ilimuua fisi."

Miguu hii yote ni ya nini? Yenyewe hainifai. Afadhali nikugawie miguu ili unirudishie angalau jicho moja. Jongoo alijaribu kumbembeleza Nyoka. Nyoka alitoroka na macho. Tangu siku hiyo Jongoo humfuata Nyoka ili amrudishie macho yake

fields.title: Mazungumzo
fields.content:
MAZUNGUMZO
Ni asubuhi. Bi. Lulu ainaingia darasani akiwa amebeba vitabu mikononi.

Bi. Lulu: Hamjambo wanafunzi?
Wanafunzi wote (kwa pamoja): Hatujambo mwalimu. Shikamoo!
Bi. Lulu: Marahaba wanafunzi. Leo nitapenda tujadiliane kuhusu usafi wa kibinafsi. Ramadhani, unaweza kutueleza maana ya usafi wa kibinafsi?
Ramadhani: Usafi wa kibinafsi ni kutunza mwili wetu ili uwe safi kila siku.
Bi. Lulu: Vizuri sana. Dida, taja njia moja ya kudumisha usafi wa kibinafsi.
Dida: Tunaoga kila siku na kunawa mikono yetu kwa maji safi.
Bi. Lulu: Hongera sana Dida. Tindilisu, kwa nini ni muhimu kuzingatia usafi wa kibinafsi?
Tindilisu: Ni muhimu kwa sababu hutusaidia kuepuka magonjwa.
Bi. Lulu: Sahihi kabisa. Ramadhani, ni wakati gani tunapaswa kunawa mikono yetu?
Ramadhani: Tunapaswa kunawa mikono kabla ya kula na baada ya kutoka msalani.
Bi. Lulu: Vizuri sana Ramadhani. Dida, taja kifaa kimoja cha usafi wa kibinafsi.
Dida: Vifaa vya kudumisha usafi wa kibinafsi ni kama vile mswaki, brashi, kikatakucha, sabuni na dodoki.
Bi. Lulu: Asante sana wanafunzi. Tukizingatia usafi wa kibinafsi, tutakuwa na afya njema daima.
Wanafunzi wote: Asante mwalimu. Kwaheri!

fields.title: Broken Passage Grade 9

We got to school __1__ than usual that morning. __2__ in our class was really excited. Even James, very unlike __3__ was shouting and laughing. On the __4__ day our class teacher had promised to take us out on a field trip. We were to __5__ a nearby forest. For __6__ of us this was a good opportunity to learn the names of trees and birds, and __7__ to pick edible fruits. The science teacher had said these were __8__ for our health.
We waited eagerly for our class teacher and kept wondering if she __9__ about the trip. However, she came on time and we set off.

fields.title: Mazungumzo
fields.content:
Soma mazungumzo yafuatayo kisha ujibu swali la 1 hadi 5.
(Saa ya mapumziko shuleni. Musa ameshika sarafu mkononi akielekea kantini. Rafiki yake, Zawadi, anamzuia kwa ishara ya mkono.)
Zawadi: (Akionyesha udadisi) Musa, mbona kila siku ya Mungu unakimbilia kantini kununua peremende na vibanzi? Je, hufikirii kuweka akiba kwa ajili ya kesho?
Musa: (Akicheka kwa dharau kidogo) Akiba? Akiba ni ya watu wazima wenye mishahara, Zawadi. Mimi ni mwanafunzi tu, hizi ni pesa za matumizi nizitumie nile vizuri!
Zawadi: (Akimakinika) Hapo unajidanganya mwehu! Hata bahari ilianza na matone. Ukizoea kutumia kila senti unayopewa leo, utakuja kuwa mtegemezi hapo baadaye.
Musa: (Akivutiwa na hoja hiyo) Kwani kuweka akiba kuna faida gani kwa mtoto kama mimi?
Zawadi: (Akisisitiza) Faida ni nyingi. Unaweza kununua vifaa vya shule unavyovipenda, au hata kumsaidia mzazi wako wakati wa dhiki. Kumbuka methali isemayo, 'haba na haba hujaza kibaba'.
Musa: (Akiingiza sarafu mfukoni) Kweli kabisa. Nimeanza kuelewa. Kuanzia leo, nitaanza kuweka nusu ya pesa yangu kwenye kibuyu changu cha akiba nyumbani.
Zawadi: (Akitabasamu) Safi sana Musa! Uamuzi wako ni kama dhahabu. Utaona matunda yake punde si punde.

fields.title: Mazungumzo
fields.content:
(Darasani wakati wa somo la Sayansi na Teknolojia. Tumaini ameshika kishikwambi (tablet) huku akimwonyesha rafiki yake, Faridi.)
Faridi: (Akistaajabu) Tumaini, mbona unashinda umekodoa macho kwenye kishikwambi hicho? Je, huchoki kucheza michezo ya video?
Tumaini: (Akitabasamu) Hasha, Faridi! Hiki si kifaa cha michezo pekee. Natumia mtandao kufanya utafiti kuhusu sayari mbalimbali. Teknolojia imerahisisha masomo sana.
Faridi: (Kwa shaka) Lakini mwalimu alisema teknolojia ni kisu chenye makali kuwili. Inaweza kutujenga au kutubomoa.
Tumaini: (Akikubali) Kweli kabisa. Ukiitumia kutazama mambo yasiyofaa au kupoteza muda, itakubomoa. Lakini ukiitumia kupata maarifa mapya, itakupa mbawa za kupaa kimasomo.
Faridi: (Akivutiwa) Kumbe! Na mimi naweza kuitumia kujifunza lugha ya kigeni?
Tumaini: Bila shaka! Dunia sasa ni kijiji kimoja. Unaweza kuwasiliana na kujifunza kutoka kwa watu wa nchi za mbali ukiwa hapa hapa darasani kwetu.
Faridi: (Akifurahi) Nimeelewa sasa. Teknolojia ni mtumishi mzuri lakini ni bwana mbaya tukiiruhusu itutawale.

fields.title: Mazungumzo (III)
fields.content:
(Uwanja wa michezo. Baraka anaonekana mwenye huzuni. Rafiki yake, Linda, anamtokea na kumpapasa bega.)
Linda: (Kwa upole) Baraka, mbona leo umevaa sura ya huzuni kama aliyefiwa na kuku wake wa pekee? Mbona huchezi na wenzako?
Baraka: (Akivuta pumzi ndefu) Ah Linda, namsikitikia jirani yangu mdogo, Bahati. Wazazi wake wanamlazimisha kwenda kuuza maji sokoni badala ya kuja shuleni.
Linda: (Akishtuka) Jamani! Hiyo si ajira ya watoto? Kila mtoto ana haki ya kupata elimu na kulindwa dhidi ya kazi ngumu.
Baraka: (Akikubali) Kweli kabisa. Bahati ana ndoto ya kuwa daktari, lakini sasa ndoto yake inaanza kufifia kwa sababu ya kubebeshwa mizigo mizito.
Linda: (Kwa msisitizo) Hatufai kunyamaza. Haki za watoto si ombi, ni sheria. Lazima tuliripoti jambo hili kwa mwalimu wa ushauri na nasaha.
Baraka: (Akifuta machozi) Je, unadhani tutamsaidia au tutawaletea wazazi wake matatizo?
Linda: (Akijibu kwa ujasiri) Tunamsaidia Bahati kupata haki yake. Mchelea mwana kulia, hulia yeye. Wazazi wake wakielimishwa, watajua kuwa kumsomesha mtoto ni kuwekeza kwenye mustakabali mwema wa jamii.
Baraka: (Akisimama) Haya Linda, twende ofisini. Tuwe sauti ya wasio na sauti.

fields.title: Jaza Pengo (I)
fields.content: Siku ya safari ya kuelekea mbuga ya wanyama ilipowadia, wanafunzi wote walifika shuleni __1__ Kila mmoja alikuwa amevalia sare safi na kubeba mkoba wake wenye mahitaji ya safari. Baada ya mwalimu mkuu kutupa nasaha fupi, tulianza __2__ kwenye basi letu kwa nidhamu ya hali ya juu. Njiani, tulijawa na __3__ tele huku tukitazama mandhari ya kuvutia ya mashamba ya kijani kibichi. Tulipowasili langoni mwa mbuga, tulianza __4__ wanyama mbalimbali kama vile tembo na simba kwa makini. Hakika, safari hiyo ilikuwa yenye __5__ kubwa kwa kila mwanafunzi.

fields.title: Jaza pengo (II)
fields.content: Siku ya michezo ilipowadia, uwanja wa shule uliopambwa kwa bendera za rangi mbalimbali ulijaa wanafunzi waliokuwa na furaha __1__. Mashindano ya mbio yalipoanza, wakimbiaji waliondoka kwenye mstari wa kuanzia kwa kasi ya __2__ huku vumbi likitimka nyuma yao. Katika mbio za mita mia nne, mwanafunzi Juma alikuwa __3__ halisi; alipita wenzake kwa hatua ndefu na kuwasili kwenye utepe wa ushindi wa kwanza. Licha ya ushindani kuwa mkali, walioshindwa walitulizwa na walimu kwa kuwakumbusha methali isemayo__ 4__. Hakika, siku hiyo ilituonyesha kuwa michezo ni __5__ katika kuimarisha afya ya miili yetu na ushirikiano shuleni.

fields.title: Ufahamu (I)
fields.content:
Siku ya mazingira duniani ilipowadia, shule yetu iliamua kufanya shughuli kabambe ya upandaji miti. Kila mwanafunzi alitakiwa kubeba mche mmoja wa mti wa matunda au kivuli. Mwalimu mkuu alitukumbusha kuwa mazingira ni urithi wetu na tukiyaangamiza, tunajiangamiza wenyewe. Alisisitiza kuwa miti ni mapafu ya nchi yetu kwani husafisha hewa na kuvuta hewa chafu.
Musa, mwanafunzi wa gredi ya sita, alikuwa mstari wa mbele. Alichimba mashimo kwa bidii huku akizingatia nafasi kati ya mti mmoja na mwingine. Rafiki yake, Zawadi, alimsaidia kutia mbolea na kumwagilia maji. Wote wawili walijifunga nira kuhakikisha kuwa kila mche uliopandwa ungestawi. Baada ya kazi hiyo ngumu, uwanja wa shule ulibadilika na kuwa na mandhari ya kuvutia. Hakika, umoja ni nguvu na utengano ni udhaifu.

fields.title: Ufahamu (ii)
fields.content:
Mchezo wa raga ulikuwa umeanza kushika kasi katika uwanja wa shule ya msingi ya Bidii. Kila upande ulijitahidi kufanya kweli ili kunyakua nishani ya dhahabu. Katika dakika za lala salama, nahodha wa timu ya 'Simba', bwana mdogo Baraka, alipokea mpira na kuanza kukimbia kama upepo kuelekea upande wa wapinzani. Mashabiki walikuwa wakishangilia kwa vifijo na vigelegele huku vumbi likitimka uwanjani.
Hata hivyo, ushindi haukuja kwa urahisi. Timu pinzani, 'Chui', ilikuwa imejipanga sawasawa kama jeshi tayari kwa mapambano. Baraka alijaribu kupita katikati yao lakini alikumbana na ukuta mgumu wa ulinzi. Aligundua kuwa hangeweza kufua dafu peke yake. Kwa busara, aliamua kumpa pasi  rafiki yake, Musa, ambaye alikuwa amejibanza pembeni. Musa alizitambua ishara zile na kwa hatua ndefu, alifanikiwa kufunga bao la ushindi. Uwanja mzima ulilipuka kwa furaha huku mwalimu wa michezo akitabasamu kwa kuridhika. Hakika, mcheza kwao hutunzwa, kwani ushindi wa huo ulichangiwa na mshikamano wa dhati.

fields.title: Ufahamu (III) - Teknolojia
fields.content:
Teknolojia imekuwa kama shilingi yenye pande mbili katika maisha ya mwanafunzi wa kisasa. Kwa upande mmoja, mtandao ni bahari ya maarifa ambapo mwanafunzi anaweza kupata habari za kisayansi, kihistoria, na hata lugha kwa mbofyo mmoja tu. Vifaa kama vishikwambi vimesaidia kurahisisha masomo darasani, huku picha na video zikifanya masomo magumu kueleweka kwa urahisi kama kumsukuma mlevi.
Hata hivyo, upande wa pili wa shilingi hii una giza totoro. Matumizi ya kupita kiasi ya vifaa hivi yamesababisha baadhi ya wanafunzi kuwa watumwa wa michezo ya video na mitandao ya kijamii. Hali hii imepelekea kudhoofika kwa nidhamu na kudorora kwa matokeo ya mitihani. Badala ya kufanya mazoezi ya darasani, wengine wanashinda wakipiga soga usiku kucha, jambo ambalo huwafanya wafike shuleni wakiwa wamechoka tiki. Ni jukumu la wazazi na walimu kuwa macho ili kuhakikisha kuwa teknolojia inatumika kama daraja la mafanikio na si shimo la maangamizi.

fields.title: The Reef of Resilience - Passage
fields.content:
In the glowing expanse of the Azure Reef, a colony of vibrant sea creatures lived in perfect harmony. Their golden rule was simple: "The current carries us all." From the smallest shrimp cleaning the coral to the giant manta rays guiding the migrations, every creature played a vital role in the reef's health.
One morning, the ocean turned unnervingly warm. A thick, dark "Silent Shadow"—a massive oil spill from a distant shipwreck—began to drift downward, choking the sunlight and poisoning the water. The reef, once a kaleidoscope of color, began to pale.
Panic bubbled through the water. Some of the swifter tuna argued that they should flee to the deeper, colder trenches and leave the slower creatures behind. But Finnegan, an old, scarred sea turtle, raised a flipper.
"If we scatter, the Shadow wins," Finnegan rasped. "We move as one, or we don't move at all."
Under Finnegan’s direction, the colony took action. The schools of parrotfish worked tirelessly to graze away the damaged algae, while the giant octopuses used their many arms to build barriers of sand and rock to deflect the grime.
The hardest moment came when they realized a nearby patch of seagrass—home to a nursery of defenseless seahorses—was completely engulfed. The reef was exhausted; could they spare the energy to help?
Finnegan didn't hesitate. "A reef is only as healthy as its weakest sprout."
The creatures formed a living chain, fanning their fins in unison to create a powerful underwater current that pushed the "Silent Shadow" away from the nursery. Weeks later, the Azure Reef didn't just recover; it sparkled brighter than ever before. The creatures had learned that while the ocean is vast and dangerous, no wave is too big when you swim together.

fields.title: The Weaver of Echoes
fields.content:
In the city of Orizon, every citizen was born with a "Life-Thread"—a glowing silk cord that pulsed with their heartbeat. The city was a massive loom where these threads were woven into a Great Tapestry. The law of the Loom was: "A snag in one is a tear in all."
For centuries, the Master Weavers kept the threads perfectly straight. They trimmed away any "errant" fibers—threads that glowed too brightly or hummed with strange rhythms—believing that uniformity was the only way to prevent the Tapestry from unraveling.
When the "Great Fray" began, it wasn't a storm or an earthquake. It was a silence. The threads began to lose their glow, turning a dull, brittle gray. The Master Weavers panicked, tightening the tension on the Loom until the threads groaned under the pressure. They believed the Tapestry was "lazy" and needed more discipline.
Mina, a young apprentice who spent her nights listening to the threads rather than pulling them, noticed something the Masters ignored. The graying threads weren't lazy; they were suffocating. They were so tightly bound that they could no longer vibrate with the "Song of the City."
Mina did the unthinkable. She didn't tighten the Loom; she took a bone-knife and cut the tension-weights.
The Masters screamed as the Tapestry slackened and sagged. But as the threads loosened, they began to breathe. They tangled, looped, and knotted in ways that were "inefficient" and "messy," but the glow returned—stronger and more colorful than before. The Tapestry was no longer a flat, perfect sheet; it was a living, multidimensional forest of light.

fields.title: Broken Passage - The Quiet Work
fields.content:
Many young people find it difficult to discover their true __1__ in life. It is not something one stumbles upon by chance — it must be actively __2__ through honest self-reflection and hard work. One must learn to silence the noise of other people's __3__ and listen to their own inner voice.

School is more than a place for passing examinations. It is a space where young minds learn to __4__ problems, collaborate with others and develop the __5__ needed to face an uncertain future. Students who treat learning as a burden often find themselves __6__ when opportunities that reward preparation finally arrive.
    Society also has a role to play. When communities choose to __7__ young people rather than ignore their potential, the results can be remarkable. A word of genuine encouragement can __8__ a young person's entire outlook and push them toward paths they never imagined possible.
    Ultimately, the difference between those who succeed and those who give up lies not in talent alone, but in __9__ Those who hold __10__ to their goals even when progress is slow are the ones who eventually make their mark.

fields.title: Club Membership
fields.content: Six learners — Grace, Omar, Linet, David, Aisha and Tom — are members of various clubs at Riverside Secondary School. Grace and Linet are active in choir and environmental club. Omar and David participate in environmental club and basketball. Aisha is involved in choir and drama. Tom is only a member of the basketball club. David and Aisha are the only ones who attend the journalism club. Grace and Omar are involved in basketball. All learners except Tom belong to at least two clubs.

fields.title: The River Speaks
fields.content:
I was born in the belly of the hill,
A whisper at first, then a roar.
I carved my path with patient will,
And knocked on every valley's door.

I carry the songs of the morning birds,
The secrets of stones worn smooth and old.
I speak a language beyond all words,
A story that has never been told.

The children laugh upon my banks,
The fishermen cast their nets at dawn.
I ask for nothing, receive no thanks,
Yet I flow on and on and on.

One day the rains will cease to fall,
My voice will thin to a tired thread.
But even then I will give my all,
Until the very last drop is shed.

fields.title: Makala: Teknolojia na Hatari ya Mmomonyoko wa Maadili
fields.content:
Katika karne hii ya ishirini na moja, dhana ya mafanikio imetafsiriwa upya na mfumo wa kibepari unaozingatia tija ya kiuchumi kuliko utu. Shule nyingi zimegeuka kuwa viwanda vya kukandika maarifa ya kinadharia vichwani mwa wanafunzi, huku zikipuuza kabisa ustadi wa kijamii na uwezo wa kuchanganua mambo kwa kina. Hali hii imezua kizazi cha wasomi ambao wana uwezo mkubwa wa kukumbuka kanuni za kisayansi, lakini wanashindwa kutoa suluhu kwa migogoro midogo ya kijamii inayowazunguka. Tunashuhudia ongezeko la vijana wenye shahada za juu ambao, licha ya uerevu wao wa vitabuni, wamebaki kuwa mateka wa fikra tegemezi.
Aidha, mmomonyoko wa maadili unaoambatana na kasi ya teknolojia si jambo la kupuuza. Badala ya teknolojia kuwa daraja la kuunganisha maarifa, imekuwa kuta inayotenganisha familia. Wazazi wamehamishia majukumu yao ya malezi kwenye vifaa vya kielektroniki, wakiamini kuwa kumpatia mtoto kishikwambi ndiyo kilele cha upendo. Matokeo yake ni vijana wanaozungumza lugha zisizo na staha na wanaofuata tamaduni za kigeni bila upembuzi yakinifu. Ni lazima mfumo wetu wa elimu urudi kwenye misingi ya malezi ya jumuiya, ambapo mwalimu, mzazi, na jamii kwa ujumla wanashirikiana kumfinyanga kijana kuwa raia mwema mwenye adili.

fields.title: Chokochoko
fields.content:
Chokochoko alibarikiwa kwa viungo vyote, ila kimoja kionekanacho kuwa kidogo kuliko vingine, kilichokuwa mfalme wa vyote. Nacho hiki kilimchongea kweli. Ama kweli, mti huchongewa na tundaze. Naye alichongewa pakubwa na ulimi wake. 

Si umbeya huo! Alikuwa kilimilimi hivi kwamba chochote alichonasa kwa yeyote awaye yule, sharti angekiwasilisha na kumtaarifu mwingine. Hata mambo ya faragha ya baraza la wazee angeingilia tu na kusambaza. Udukizi wa aina hii ulimpelekea kuwa na rabsha baina yake na wote waliomzunguka. Msemo "funika kombe mwanaharamu apite" ukawa unamlenga yeye. 

Waaidha ulimi wake ulikuwa ulimi wa upanga. Kungalikuwa na mashindano ya kukaripiana, neno la kwanza la Chokochoko ndilo lingalimpiga dafrao mshindani wake hata ajiuzulu mara moja. Maneno yake yote yalikuwa mfano wa sumu ya joka kali. Naye kwake, hili lilikuwa na hasara pia - alivaa uso usiofurahi, kila wakati umekunja peto.

 Chokochoko pia alikuwa mcheza shere. Ukweli ukawa neno lisilo na maana kwake. Hakuiona ahadi kuwa deni. Yumkini mtu kama huyu huveka nadhiri hata kwa Mungu asiitimize, huyo thawabu ataipataje? 

Uchokochoko wake ulimfanya awe ndumakuwili. Alikuwa kisebusebu. Akimwona yeyote akinawiri angejitokeza kama aliyeteremea mno na mwenye kusherehekea pamoja na apataye. Hili lingemfanya aliyelengwa kumfunulia moyo wake. Maadui wangevutwa kwa ulimi. Kuna walioibiwa, kutapeliwa, kuuawa na dhuluma nyinginezo kutokana na hila zake. Kumbe alitaka kufaidika yeye. 

Naye avumaye baharini papa kumbe wengine wapo. Aliwabughudhi vijana wengi kwa muda mrefu nao wakampangia njama, wakaitumia njia ile ile kulipiza kisasi.

 Licha ya kuutumia ulimi kuropoka, aliutumia pia kupiga maji. Siku ya mtego, vijana hao walijifanya mkono wazi wakamnunulia kileo. Alipokwisha kuchapa ugimbi, vijana wakaanza kumsifu Chokochoko kwa ujanja huku wakiwakashifu watu mbalimbali waliojua walikwisha hasiriwa na afriti huyu. Kusikia sifa zake zainuka, alianza kutokwa na maneno bila ya mpango, huku akieleza namna alivyowashusha wote hadhi hao kwa jinsi mbalimbali; kuna aliowapangia kuuawa, kuibiwa, kuchomewa nyumba na kadhalika. 

Wakati wote huu, kachero mmoja alikwisha karibia akiwa na kijiredio cha kunasa sauti. Akishaboboka bobobo, makachero wawili wakajitokeza na kumfahamisha shughuli zao pale. Alipojaribu kujitetea kiredio kikafunguliwa, akayasikia yote aliyosimulia. Akaleuka palepale, akawaangalia wale vijana majirani ambao sasa walikuwa wakitabasamu.

fields.title: Jaza Pengo | 1
fields.content: Binadamu wote walioumbwa na Mungu __1__. Hakuna anayepaswa kujiona __2__ wengine. Tabia hii huitwa __3__. Hata __4__ kuwa utajaliwa ufanisi __5__ maishani, kamwe usiwadharau wengine __6__ ukumbuke msemo wa wahenga kuwa __7__. Mja akikulilia hali wakati wowote, usisite __8__ kadri uwezavyo. Baraka anazokumiminia Maulana hutegemea namna __9__ wengine hasa wenye mahitaji yao.

fields.title: Jaza pengo | 2
fields.content: Uandishi wa insha yoyote __1__ mwanafunzi kuwa makini. Kwanza, anahitajika kuelewa __2__ sana matakwa ya swali __3__. Aidha __4__ yanayojitokeza yanapaswa kuwa yenye uzito. __5__ kama vile uadilifu, UKIMWI, majanga, teknolojia, haki na ajira za watoto __6__ uzito insha yake.

fields.title: Jaza pengo | 3
fields.content: Majuto aliketi na kutulia __1__ huku mawazo __2__ akilini __3__. Ni mwezi __4__ tangu ajipate katika __5__ cha kurekebisha tabia. __6__ huyu alianza kutumia mihadarati alipokuwa katika darasa la sita. __7__ miaka miwili alirukwa na __8__ akawa hajijui __9__. Darasani aliongea peke yake. Mambo yalikuwa yameenda __10__.

fields.title: Ushairi | 1
fields.content:
Elimu ni ngao yetu, gizani inatutoa,
Ni nuru ya moyo wetu, milango inafungua,
Tusome kwa bidii yetu, malengo kuyatua,
Bila maarifa mwanetu, maisha yatatuboa.

Walimu kioo chetu, kwa upendo wanatufunza,
Nidhamu ni nguzo yetu, heshima kuiongeza,
Vitabu marafiki wetu, busara wanatupunza,
Kufaulu ni jasho letu, sifa tutazitangaza.

Mazingira ya shule, yawe safi kila saa,
Tusiwe na lile nile, la kutupa taka jaa,
Tuzikate nyasi kule, wadudu wasije kaa,
Afya ni bora kuliko, mali inayong'aa.

Teknolojia mpya, ni katu ya maendeleo,
Tutumie kwa nia, tusiwe na mazoea,
Maarifa ya kidijitali, ni hitaji la leo,
Dunia sasa ni kijiji, tusibaki na kilio.

Tuungane sote jamani, tujenge nchi yetu,
Uzalendo mioyoni, ndio uungwana wetu,
Amani na usalama, ndio fahari ya kwetu,
Kenya yetu ya kishujaa, ni urithi wa vizazi vyetu.

fields.question_text: Grade 8 learners were being taught about soil pollution control. The following are some of the ways soil is polluted except:-
fields.option_c: Dumping of plastic waste in the farm
fields.explanation: Practicing crop rotation is a soil conservation practice, not a way soil is polluted. It helps maintain soil fertility and prevents soil degradation.

fields.question_text: A teacher taught the students the reasons why we should conserve water in the soil. Which one of the following is a reason?
fields.option_a: To make plants grow at a slower rate
fields.option_b: Because it will not rain again
fields.option_c: To ensure constant supply of water to the plants
fields.option_d: To help mosquitoes and frogs breed
fields.explanation: Water conservation in soil ensures a constant supply of water to plants, which is essential for their growth and survival.

fields.question_text: Mulching is done using the following materials. Which one of the materials is it?
fields.explanation: Leaves and grass are organic materials commonly used for mulching to retain soil moisture and suppress weeds.

fields.question_text: Identify the type of seeds that will germinate easily in the list below.
fields.explanation: Mature seeds have fully developed embryos and stored nutrients, making them most likely to germinate successfully.

fields.question_text: All the following are planting sites found in our school environment except one. Which is it?
fields.explanation: A long fence is not a planting site but a structure. Ground sites, underground, and container sites are all valid planting locations.

fields.question_text: State 3 examples of non-flowering plants
Explain why chamber B has thicker muscular wall than chamber C.
fields.correct_answer: Chamber B (left ventricle) has thicker muscular walls than chamber C (right ventricle) because it needs to pump blood to the entire body through systemic circulation, requiring higher pressure, while chamber C only pumps blood to the lungs through pulmonary circulation which requires lower pressure.
fields.explanation: The left ventricle needs more muscle to generate higher pressure for systemic circulation

fields.question_text: State two adaptations of red blood cells to their functions.
fields.correct_answer: 1. Biconcave shape increases surface area for oxygen transport. 2. Lack of nucleus provides more space for haemoglobin. 3. Flexible membrane allows passage through narrow capillaries. 4. Contains haemoglobin for oxygen binding (any two adaptations)
fields.explanation: Red blood cells have specific structural adaptations for efficient oxygen transport

fields.question_text: Name the type of immunity produced when antibodies from the mother are passed to the foetus during breastfeeding.
fields.explanation: Passive immunity occurs when antibodies are transferred from mother to child rather than being produced by the child's immune system

fields.question_text: A two digit number is such that when the digits are reversed, the value of the number increases by 36. If the sum of the unit digit and twice the tens digit is 16, find the number.
fields.explanation: Let the number be 10A + B where A is tens digit and B is unit digit. When reversed: 10B + A. Given: (10B + A) - (10A + B) = 36, which gives 9B - 9A = 36, so B - A = 4. Also given: B + 2A = 16. Solving: A = 4, B = 8, so the number is 48.

fields.question_text: Simplify 27^x ÷ 2^4 / 32^(1/5) leaving your answer as a simple fraction
fields.explanation: The student worked through the problem step by step, converting to (3 ÷ 16) / 2, then multiplying 3/16 × 1/2 = 3/32

fields.question_text: Who among the following prophets of Allah married his daughter to Nabi Musa (a.s)?
fields.explanation: Prophet Shuaib married his daughter to Prophet Musa (a.s) after Musa helped his daughters at the well

fields.question_text: Possessor of two lights was a title given to caliph
fields.explanation: Caliph Uthman was called 'Possessor of two lights' because he married two daughters of Prophet Muhammad

fields.question_text: The prophet agreed to visit Makkah the following year for Umrah because
fields.option_a: the sawahabas advised him to do so
fields.option_c: the Quraish had a stonger army
fields.option_d: he was not prepared for war
fields.explanation: The Prophet loved peace and made the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah to avoid conflict

fields.question_text: Which month do muslims commemorate milad un Nabi?
fields.explanation: Milad un Nabi (Prophet's birthday) is commemorated in Rabiul Awwal

fields.question_text: The prophet (P.B.U.H) took cover in cave Thaur on his trip to
fields.explanation: The Prophet took cover in cave Thaur during the Hijra (migration) to Madina

fields.question_text: The following are names of Islamic garments. Which one is not?
fields.explanation: Visco cotton is a type of fabric material, not a specific Islamic garment

fields.question_text: Suratul Qadr teaches us that the night of power is better than
fields.explanation: Suratul Qadr states that Laylatul Qadr is better than a thousand months

fields.question_text: What is the purpose of Allah creating human beings?
fields.explanation: Allah created humans primarily to worship Him as stated in the Quran

fields.question_text: The mother of Nabii Musa (a.s) was given _____ assurances.
fields.explanation: The mother of Prophet Musa was given 2 assurances: that he would be returned to her and made a messenger

fields.question_text: Who among the following was the second khalifah of Islam?
fields.explanation: Umar ibn Al-Khattab was the second Caliph after Abu Bakr

fields.question_text: Minor Hajj is called
fields.explanation: Umra is the minor pilgrimage, also called minor Hajj

fields.question_text: The act of mixing good quality with low quality in goods Islam is called
fields.explanation: Ghush refers to fraud or cheating in trade by mixing good and bad quality goods

fields.question_text: Whoever believes in Allah and the last day should not annoy his
fields.explanation: Islamic teaching emphasizes not annoying one's neighbor as a sign of faith

fields.question_text: In suratul Qadr Allah (S.W.T) refers to 'Ruh' as angel
fields.explanation: In Suratul Qadr, 'Ruh' refers to Angel Jibril (Gabriel)

fields.question_text: Prophet Musa worked as a _____ for the father of the two women he helped.
fields.explanation: Prophet Musa worked as a shepherd for Prophet Shuaib after helping his daughters

fields.question_text: The surah which encourages us to sacrifice on idd day is
fields.explanation: Surah Al-Kauthar encourages sacrifice and prayer, traditionally associated with Eid sacrifice

fields.question_text: Which among the following is sunnah fast day?
fields.explanation: Thursday is one of the recommended (sunnah) days for voluntary fasting

fields.question_text: The grandfather of the prophet was
fields.explanation: Abdul Muttalib was the grandfather of Prophet Muhammad (SAW)

fields.question_text: After the conquest of makkah Al-kaaba has _____ idols which were broken.
fields.explanation: There were 360 idols in the Kaaba that were destroyed after the conquest of Makkah

fields.question_text: The prophet who spoke at very tender age was
fields.explanation: Prophet Issa (Jesus) spoke as a baby in the cradle defending his mother

fields.question_text: The prophet (S.A.W) said that the number of people who get sin from alcohol are
fields.explanation: According to hadith, ten categories of people are cursed in relation to alcohol

fields.question_text: The area of a sector is 11 cm². If the radius of the sector is 3 cm, calculate the angle subtended by the sector?
fields.explanation: Using the formula Area of sector = θ/360 × πr², substitute the given values: 11 = θ/360 × π × 3². Solving for θ: 11 = θ/360 × 9π, therefore θ = (11 × 360)/(9π). Using π = 22/7, θ = (11 × 360 × 7)/(9 × 22) = 140°

fields.question_text: A bag contains 5 red balls and 3 blue balls. (1) Two balls are drawn one after the other without replacement. Find the probability that both balls are red. (2) The balls are returned to the bag after each draw. Two balls are drawn with replacement. Find the probability that both balls are red.
fields.explanation: Without replacement: P(both red) = (5/8) × (4/7) = 20/56 = 5/14. With replacement: P(both red) = (5/8) × (5/8) = 25/64.

fields.question_text: AB is the diameter of the semi-circle. Find the area of the shaded region π = 22/7
fields.explanation: Area of semicircle = (1/2)πr² = (1/2) × (22/7) × (8.5)² = 113.125 cm². Area of triangle ABC = (1/2) × base × height = (1/2) × 17 × 8 = 68 cm². Using Pythagorean theorem with AC = 15 cm and AB = 17 cm, we get BC = 8 cm. Height from C to AB = 8 cm. Shaded area = Area of semicircle - Area of triangle = 113.125 - 68 = 45.125 cm². However, given the measurements shown, the shaded area appears to be the segment, which equals approximately 14 cm².

fields.question_text: Water flows from a pipe at the rate of 250 litres per minute. If the pipe is used to drain a tank full of water measuring 3.2m by 2.5m by 2m, how many minutes would it take to drain the tank completely?
fields.explanation: Volume of tank = 3.2 × 2.5 × 2 = 16 m³ = 16,000 litres. Time = 16,000 ÷ 250 = 64 minutes

fields.question_text: Water is flowing through a cylindrical pipe at the speed of 1.2m/s. If the pipe has an internal radius of 1.4cm, Calculate the volume of water delivered by the pipe per second in cm³ (Take π = 22/7)
fields.explanation: Volume per second = π × r² × speed = (22/7) × 1.4² × 1.2 × 100 = 739.2 cm³

fields.question_text: The depth to which the pipe fills a rectangular tank of base dimensions 1.5m x 2m in one hour to the nearest 0.1cm.
fields.explanation: Volume in 1 hour = 739.2 × 3600 cm³. Depth = Volume ÷ base area = (739.2 × 3600) ÷ (150 × 200) = 88.7cm

fields.question_text: The time taken, to the nearest second for the pipe to fill a 50 litre bath tub (initially empty) which has a hole at the base that drains the tub at the rate of 3 litres per minute.
fields.explanation: Net filling rate = 739.2 - 50 = 689.2 cm³/s. Time = 50,000 ÷ 689.2 = 72.548 ≈ 73 seconds

fields.question_text: Pipe x can fill an empty tank in 3 hours while pipe y can fill the same tank in 6 hours. When the tank is full, it can be emptied by pipe z in 8 hours. Pipe x and y are opened at the same time when the tank is empty. If one hour later pipe z is also opened, find the total time taken to fill the tank.
fields.explanation: First hour: x and y fill 1/3 + 1/6 = 1/2 of tank. Remaining 1/2 tank filled by all three pipes at rate (1/3 + 1/6 - 1/8) = 9/24 per hour. Time for remaining = (1/2) ÷ (9/24) = 4/3 hours. Total = 1 + 4/3 = 2⅓ hours

fields.question_text: Evaluate $\frac{2^4}{32^{\frac{1}{5}}}$
Vivian is using the device below to cut a piece of metal. What is the name of the tool?
fields.explanation: A hacksaw is specifically designed for cutting metal materials

fields.question_text: A safety officer outlined possible causes of fire as shown below. Which one is not a potential cause?
Rita has bought the hardware shown below from the shop. Identify the name of the hardware.
After occurrence of fire in a certain occasion, Philip used a method which involved cutting off oxygen supply. What is the name given to such method?
fields.explanation: Smothering is the fire suppression method that involves cutting off oxygen supply to extinguish fire

fields.question_text: Digital devices are affected by a variety of malwares. Which of the following is a type of malware that disguises itself as a legitimate program?
fields.explanation: A Trojan Horse is malware that disguises itself as legitimate software to trick users into installing it

fields.question_text: The tool below is known as?
What is the surface area of a sphere whose diameter is 20m? ($\pi = 3.142$)
fields.explanation: Use the formula for surface area of a sphere: $A = 4\pi r^2$. Given diameter = 20m, so radius = 10m. Substitute: $A = 4 \times 3.142 \times 10^2 = 4 \times 3.142 \times 100 = 1256.8 \text{ m}^2$

fields.question_text: Simplify $\frac{x+3}{2} + \frac{y-3}{2}$
fields.explanation: Combine fractions with same denominator: $\frac{x+3}{2} + \frac{y-3}{2} = \frac{(x+3)+(y-3)}{2} = \frac{x+3+y-3}{2} = \frac{x+y}{2}$

fields.question_text: Simplify $\frac{4x-3}{4} + \frac{-3y+4}{3}$
fields.explanation: Find common denominator of 12, then combine: $\frac{4x-3}{4} + \frac{-3y+4}{3} = \frac{3(4x-3)}{12} + \frac{4(-3y+4)}{12} = \frac{12x-9-12y+16}{12} = \frac{12x-12y+7}{12}$

fields.question_text: Find the square root of 4898
fields.explanation: Calculate $\sqrt{4898}$ using appropriate method

fields.question_text: Find the length of each side of a square carpet whose area is equal to the area of a rectangular carpet of sides 0.7m by 0.39m
fields.explanation: Area of rectangle = 0.7 × 0.39 = 0.273 m². For square with same area, side length = $\sqrt{0.273} \approx 0.522$ m

fields.question_text: The gradient of the line joining the points P(2, -8) and Q(1, y) is -4. Find the value of y
fields.explanation: Using the gradient formula: $m = \frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1}$. So $-4 = \frac{y - (-8)}{1 - 2} = \frac{y + 8}{-1}$. Therefore $-4 = -(y + 8)$, which gives $-4 = -y - 8$, so $y = -4$

fields.question_text: Evaluate $\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{-3}$.
fields.explanation: Using the rule $a^{-n} = \frac{1}{a^n}$: $\left(\frac{3}{2}\right)^{-3} = \left(\frac{2}{3}\right)^{3} = \frac{2^3}{3^3} = \frac{8}{27}$

fields.question_text: Factorise completely $2ax + 4ay - 3bx - 6by$.
fields.explanation: Group terms: $2a(x + 2y) - 3b(x + 2y) = (2a - 3b)(x + 2y)$

fields.question_text: Simplify $3(4x - 5) + 2$.
Find the value of $\log_{10}100$
Find the value of $\log_{10}1$
Find the value of $\log_{10}0.1$
Find the value of $\log_{10}1000$
If three coins are tossed, what is the probability of getting exactly one head?
fields.explanation: When three coins are tossed, there are 8 possible outcomes (2³ = 8). The favorable outcomes for exactly one head are: HTT, THT, TTH. So probability = $\frac{3}{8}$

fields.question_text: A pail is in the shape of frustum with base radius 6cm and top radius 8cm. The vertical height of the pail is 30cm as shown. The pail is full of water. Calculate the volume of the water in cm³, leaving your answer to 2 decimal places ($\pi = \frac{22}{7}$)
fields.explanation: Using similar triangles: $\frac{6}{8} = \frac{h}{h+30}$, solve to get h = 90cm, so H = 120cm. Volume of frustum = Volume of large cone - Volume of small cone = $\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 8^2 \times 120 - \frac{1}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 6^2 \times 90 = 8045\frac{5}{7} - 3394\frac{2}{7} = 4651\frac{3}{7}$ cm³

fields.question_text: In oblique projection, what is the relationship between the front face of the object and the projection plane?
fields.option_a: The front face is parallel to the projection plane.
fields.option_b: The front face is at a 45-degree angle to the plane.
fields.option_c: The front face is perpendicular to the projection plane.
fields.option_d: The front face is tilted at a random angle.
fields.explanation: In oblique projection, the front face is always parallel to the projection plane, which allows it to maintain its true shape and size.

fields.question_text: Which type of oblique projection shows the receding axes at their full, true length?
fields.explanation: Cavalier projection shows the receding axes at their full, true length (scale ratio 1:1), while Cabinet projection uses a reduced scale.

fields.question_text: In Cabinet projection, if an object has a real depth of 10 cm, how long should the receding line be drawn?
fields.explanation: In Cabinet projection, the receding lines are drawn at half the actual depth, so 10 cm becomes 5 cm.

fields.question_text: What are the most common angles used for the receding lines (oblique axes) in these drawings?
fields.explanation: The most common angles for receding lines in oblique projection are 30 and 45 degrees to the horizontal.

fields.question_text: Why is oblique projection often preferred for objects with circular features on one face?
fields.option_a: Circles on the front face remain true circles and are not distorted into ellipses.
fields.option_b: It automatically calculates the perspective of the curve.
fields.option_c: It makes circles look three-dimensional without shading.
fields.option_d: It is the only method that allows for curved lines.
fields.explanation: In oblique projection, circles on the front face remain as true circles because the front face is parallel to the projection plane, avoiding distortion.

fields.question_text: Which of the following is a disadvantage of Cavalier projection?
fields.option_a: It is too difficult to measure the depth.
fields.option_b: The object appears elongated or "too deep" to the human eye.
fields.option_d: It cannot be used for mechanical parts.
fields.explanation: Cavalier projection shows the full depth, which can make objects appear unnaturally elongated or too deep to the human eye.

fields.question_text: In an oblique drawing, the vertical lines of the object are drawn:
fields.explanation: In oblique projection, vertical lines remain vertical (90 degrees to the horizontal) to maintain the true shape of the front face.

fields.question_text: What determines whether an oblique projection is 'Cavalier' or 'Cabinet'?
fields.option_a: The angle of the receding axis.
fields.option_b: The scale ratio used for the depth axis.
fields.option_c: The number of vanishing points used.
fields.option_d: The complexity of the object being drawn.
fields.explanation: The difference between Cavalier and Cabinet projection is the scale ratio used for the depth axis - Cavalier uses 1:1, Cabinet uses 1:2.

fields.question_text: Which statement is true regarding all parallel projections, including oblique?
fields.option_a: Parallel lines on the object remain parallel in the drawing.
fields.option_b: All lines are drawn at half their actual length.
fields.option_c: The drawing always shows three faces of the object equally.
fields.option_d: The projection lines converge at the eye of the observer.
fields.explanation: In all parallel projections, including oblique projection, parallel lines on the object remain parallel in the drawing.

fields.question_text: When choosing which face to place as the 'front' in an oblique drawing, what is the best practice?
fields.option_a: Place the simplest face at the front.
fields.option_b: Place the longest dimension or the most complex features at the front.
fields.option_c: Always place the top of the object as the front.
fields.option_d: The choice of front face does not matter.
fields.explanation: The best practice is to place the longest dimension or most complex features at the front to show the most detail and minimize distortion.

fields.question_text: What is the first step in wood preparation after a tree has been felled?
fields.explanation: Debarking and delimbing prepares the log for transport and the initial sawing process.

cached_ai_explanation.feedback:
Not quite. The right answer is: Option B: Debarking and Delimbing.
Seasoning comes much later in the wood preparation process.
Debarking and delimbing prepares the log for transport and the initial sawing process.
This must happen first before any other processing can begin.
Remember: Think about what needs to be done immediately after cutting down the tree.
cached_ai_explanation.study_tip: Picture the tree right after it falls - it still has bark and branches that must be removed before anything else can happen.

fields.question_text: In wood conversion, which sawing method involves cutting logs into parallel boards along the entire length?
fields.explanation: This is the simplest and most economical way to produce boards using parallel cuts.

cached_ai_explanation.feedback:
Not quite. The right answer is: Option A: Plain or Through-and-Through Sawing.

Quarter sawing is a different method that cuts the log into quarters first, then cuts each quarter at an angle to the growth rings. Plain or Through-and-Through Sawing involves cutting logs into parallel boards along the entire length, which is the simplest and most economical way to produce boards using parallel cuts.

Remember: Plain sawing creates parallel cuts straight through the log from one end to the other.
cached_ai_explanation.study_tip: Think of plain sawing like slicing bread - you make straight, parallel cuts all the way through from one end to the other.

fields.question_text: Which wood seasoning method utilizes a controlled environment with specific temperature and humidity settings?
fields.explanation: Kilns use heaters and fans to precisely control the drying process for faster results.

cached_ai_explanation.feedback:
Not quite. The right answer is: Option C: Kiln Seasoning.
Water seasoning involves soaking wood in water, not controlling temperature and humidity. Kiln seasoning uses heaters and fans to precisely control the drying process for faster results.
This method creates a controlled environment where both temperature and humidity can be set to specific levels.
Remember: Kiln seasoning is the only method that gives you complete control over the drying conditions.
cached_ai_explanation.study_tip: Think of a kiln like an oven for wood - you can set exactly how hot and how dry you want it inside.

fields.question_text: What is the primary purpose of wood seasoning?
fields.option_a: To change the color of the wood
fields.option_c: To increase the weight of the timber
fields.option_d: To make the wood more flexible
fields.explanation: Reducing moisture prevents warping, shrinking, and rot while increasing strength.

cached_ai_explanation.feedback:
Correct! You chose Option B: To reduce the moisture content, which is exactly right.
Wood seasoning is the process of removing excess water from timber to make it suitable for use.
Reducing moisture prevents warping, shrinking, and rot while increasing strength.
This makes the wood more stable and durable for construction and other uses.
Remember: Seasoned wood is stronger and less likely to change shape after being used in projects.
cached_ai_explanation.study_tip: Think of wood seasoning like drying wet clothes - you remove water to make them ready to use properly.

fields.question_text:
Why was education considered a luxury in this
village?
fields.option_a: The villagers believed farming was more important.
fields.option_c: The government did not allow education.
fields.option_d: The village had plenty of educated people.

fields.question_text: A learner walks through the school compound and sees a white cotton-like growth on a piece of bread. What type of fungus is this most likely to be?
fields.explanation: White cotton-like growth on bread is characteristic of mould

fields.question_text: During a nature walk, the class finds a structure with a stalk and cap growing near a tree. Which fungus are they observing?
fields.explanation: A structure with a stalk and cap is characteristic of a toadstool

fields.question_text: Which organism among the following is not a fungus?
fields.explanation: Grass is a plant, not a fungus

fields.question_text: Fungi help break down dead plants and animals. Why is this role important in nature?
fields.option_b: It returns nutrients to the soil
fields.explanation: Decomposition by fungi returns nutrients to the soil for other organisms to use

fields.question_text: Yeast is commonly used in baking because it:
fields.explanation: Yeast produces carbon dioxide gas which makes bread rise

fields.question_text: Which of the following is an economic importance of fungi?
fields.option_c: Helping in fermentation to make products
fields.explanation: Fungi are economically important in fermentation processes to make various products

fields.question_text: A student places slices of bread in two bags: Bag A: Sealed tightly, Bag B: Left slightly open. After 3 days, mould grows faster in Bag B. Which condition MOST likely caused this difference?
fields.explanation: Air availability provides oxygen and fungal spores needed for mould growth

fields.question_text: If fungi did not exist in the environment, what is the MOST likely consequence?
fields.option_b: Accumulation of dead plants and animals
fields.explanation: Without fungi as decomposers, dead organic matter would accumulate

fields.question_text: A class experiment shows that food left uncovered spoils faster. Which statement explains this best?
fields.option_b: Fungi cannot grow on uncovered food
fields.option_c: Fungi spores in the air land easily on uncovered food
fields.option_d: Fungi only grow on cold food
fields.explanation: Uncovered food is exposed to airborne fungal spores which cause spoilage

fields.question_text: Why should learners avoid touching unknown mushrooms?
fields.explanation: Unknown mushrooms may contain toxic or harmful substances

fields.question_text: Which is the safest way to dispose mouldy food in school?
fields.option_c: Burning it or placing it in a closed waste bin
fields.explanation: Proper disposal prevents spread of mould spores and contamination

fields.question_text: Explain why fungi are classified as living things even though they do not make their own food.
fields.correct_answer: They grow, reproduce, respond to the environment, and obtain nutrients from other materials
fields.explanation: Living things are characterized by growth, reproduction, response to stimuli, and nutrition - fungi exhibit all these characteristics

fields.question_text: A learner observes that mould grows faster on bread than on dry beans. Give TWO scientific reasons for this observation.
fields.correct_answer: Bread has more moisture; Bread has more nutrients available; Bread is softer and easier for fungi to break down
fields.explanation: Fungi require moisture and nutrients to grow, and softer substrates are easier to decompose

fields.question_text: Describe TWO ways fungi contribute positively to human life.
fields.correct_answer: Used in baking (yeast); Used in fermentation (e.g., yoghurt); Some fungi are food (e.g., mushrooms); Used in medicine (antibiotics)
fields.explanation: Fungi have multiple beneficial uses in food production, medicine, and industry

fields.question_text: A Grade 6 learner observed a creature in the school garden with a segmented body and many pairs of legs (two pairs per segment). The creature curled into a spiral when touched. This animal is MOST likely a:
fields.explanation: Millipedes have segmented bodies with two pairs of legs per segment and curl into a spiral when threatened, unlike centipedes which have one pair of legs per segment.

fields.question_text: While exploring the rocky shore of the Indian Ocean, Amina found an animal with eight arms and a soft body. Why is this animal classified as an invertebrate?
fields.option_b: It has the ability to camouflage.
fields.option_d: It has a high economic value as food.
fields.explanation: Invertebrates are classified as animals that lack an internal backbone or vertebral column.

fields.question_text: Which of the following pairs consists of invertebrates that are both beneficial to a farmer in terms of soil health and pollination?
fields.explanation: Earthworms improve soil health through aeration and nutrient addition, while honeybees provide pollination services.

fields.question_text: During a nature walk, a teacher advised students NOT to touch a certain hairy caterpillar. Which safety precaution was the teacher emphasizing?
fields.option_a: Protecting the insect from human heat.
fields.option_b: Preventing skin irritation or allergic reactions.
fields.explanation: Hairy caterpillars can cause skin irritation or allergic reactions when touched due to their defensive hairs.

fields.question_text: A learner saw a slug and a snail in a damp area of the school garden. Which of the following is a SIMILARITY between the two animals?
fields.option_a: Both have a hard outer shell for protection.
fields.option_b: Both belong to the group of invertebrates with soft bodies.
fields.option_c: Both have jointed legs for fast movement.
fields.option_d: Both are used by humans for pollination.
fields.explanation: Both slugs and snails are mollusks with soft bodies, though only snails have shells.

fields.question_text: While cleaning the classroom, Juma was bitten by a spider. What is the most appropriate FIRST AID action he should take?
fields.option_a: Ignore it because spiders are small.
fields.option_b: Kill the spider and throw it away immediately.
fields.option_c: Wash the area with clean water and soap and inform the teacher.
fields.option_d: Apply strong chemicals to the bite area to kill the venom.
fields.explanation: The proper first aid for a spider bite is to clean the wound and seek help from an adult.

fields.question_text: Which of the following invertebrates is CORRECTLY matched with its importance in the environment?
fields.option_b: Bees — Making the soil fertile through aeration.
fields.option_c: Butterflies — Helping plants to produce seeds through pollination.
fields.option_d: Centipedes — Providing silk for making clothes.
fields.explanation: Butterflies are important pollinators that help plants reproduce by transferring pollen between flowers.

fields.question_text: A group of learners found an invertebrate with a hard exoskeleton and many jointed legs in a pond. Which of the following is it likely to be?
fields.explanation: Crabs have hard exoskeletons and jointed legs, and can be found in aquatic environments.

fields.question_text: Which of the following invertebrates is a parasite that commonly affects the health of livestock in Kenya?
fields.explanation: Mites are parasitic invertebrates that commonly affect livestock health by feeding on their blood or causing skin irritation.

fields.question_text: Why are Earthworms often called "the farmer's best friend"?
fields.option_a: They eat pests that destroy crops.
fields.option_b: They make the soil loose and add nutrients.
fields.option_c: They produce honey for the farmer to sell.
fields.option_d: They protect the farm from intruders.
fields.explanation: Earthworms improve soil structure by aerating it and add nutrients through their castings.

fields.question_text: Which pair of invertebrates consists ONLY of animals found in the sea?
fields.explanation: Both octopus and starfish are exclusively marine invertebrates.

fields.question_text: When handling invertebrates during a Science lesson, why is it important to use a pair of forceps or gloves?
fields.option_b: To prevent the animal from escaping.
fields.option_c: To protect yourself from stings or harmful body fluids.
fields.option_d: To make the animal look more interesting.
fields.explanation: Using forceps or gloves protects students from potential stings, bites, or harmful secretions from invertebrates.

fields.question_text: A student observed an invertebrate with 8 legs and no wings. This animal cannot be an insect because:
fields.explanation: Insects are characterized by having exactly 6 legs, so an 8-legged animal cannot be an insect.

fields.question_text: Which of the following is a way of controlling harmful invertebrates like mosquitoes without damaging the environment?
fields.option_a: Spraying heavy oil in all forests.
fields.option_b: Clearing stagnant water around the house.
fields.option_c: Burning all the grass in the community.
fields.option_d: Using plastic covers on all trees.
fields.explanation: Removing stagnant water eliminates mosquito breeding sites without harming the environment.

fields.question_text: State 1 adaptive feature and 2 characteristics of the animal shown below.
fields.correct_answer: Answers will vary based on the animal shown in the image
fields.explanation: Students should identify one adaptive feature (how the animal is suited to its environment) and two general characteristics of the invertebrate shown.

fields.question_text: Kanusha sentensi: Mgeni ameenda sebuleni.
fields.explanation: Kukanusha sentensi ya wakati uliopita 'ameenda' ni 'hajaenda'

fields.question_text: Makao ya kuku ni
fields.explanation: Kuku huishi katika kichuguu

fields.question_text: Tumia -ingine: Amemuuza gari
fields.explanation: Gari ni ngeli ya JI-MA, kwa hiyo tunatumia 'lingine'

fields.question_text: Mtoto wa mjomba au shangazi ni
fields.explanation: Mtoto wa mjomba au shangazi huitwa binamu

fields.question_text: Neno, 'Kipepeo' liko katika ngeli gani?
fields.explanation: Kipepeo (umoja) - vipepeo (wingi), kwa hiyo ni ngeli ya KI-VI

fields.question_text: Kifaa kinachotumiwa kukunia nazi kinaitwa?
fields.explanation: Kinu ni kifaa cha kukunia nazi

fields.question_text: Kamilisha sentensi: Alifika mapema____hakumpata.
fields.explanation: 'Lakini' ni kiunganishi kinachonyesha utofauti

fields.question_text: Kinyume cha neno 'giza' ni
fields.explanation: Kinyume cha giza ni mwangaza

fields.question_text: Chagua wingi wa: Karatasi hii imetulika.
fields.explanation: Wingi wa karatasi ni makaratasi, na kupatana na kivumishi 'hizi' na kitenzi 'zimetulika'

fields.question_text: Kamilisha methali: Mgema akisifiwa____
fields.explanation: Methali kamili ni 'Mgema akisifiwa huasiwa guu'

fields.question_text: Asirrimu '1/4' kwa maneno ni
fields.explanation: 1/4 ni robo kwa Kiswahili

fields.question_text: Gani kati ya viumbe hawa ni ndege?
fields.explanation: Kanga ni ndege, mengine ni wanyamapori

fields.question_text: Umbo hili ni gani?
fields.explanation: Kulingana na umbo lililoonyeshwa

fields.question_text: Kamilisha sentensi: Wanafunzi____walituzwa
fields.explanation: Wahodari ni sifa inayopatana na wingi wa wanafunzi

fields.question_text: Timia enye kwa usahihi: Mti____matunda umekauka.
fields.explanation: Mti ni ngeli ya M-MI, kwa hiyo tunatumia 'lenye'

fields.question_text: In a certain event, the ratio of teachers to students to parents is 2 : 5 : 1. If there are a total of 56 people at the event
Which of the following mammals does not give birth?
fields.explanation: Spiny ant eater (echidna) is a monotreme mammal that lays eggs instead of giving birth to live young

fields.question_text: Conifers reproduce by means of __________
fields.explanation: Conifers are gymnosperm plants that reproduce by means of cones which contain seeds

fields.question_text: Three of the following are uses of light except one. Which one is it?
fields.explanation: Ironing clothes uses heat energy, not light energy. Photography, photosynthesis (making plant food), and communication all use light

fields.question_text: In the human breathing system, gaseous exchange takes place in the __________
fields.explanation: Gaseous exchange (oxygen and carbon dioxide exchange) takes place in the air sacs (alveoli) of the lungs

fields.question_text: Grade 5 learners were learning about reproduction in plants. Which part of the plant was appropriate for the study?
fields.explanation: The flower is the reproductive organ of plants and contains all the parts necessary for plant reproduction

fields.question_text: What is the function of the part marked Q?
fields.explanation: The petals (part Q) are colorful and attract insects for pollination

fields.question_text: The process of grouping plants with common characteristics together is called __________
fields.explanation: Classification is the scientific process of grouping living things with similar characteristics together

fields.question_text: Which group of plants consists of non-flowering plants only?
fields.explanation: Moss, fern, and cypress are all non-flowering plants. The other options contain flowering plants

fields.question_text: From the experiment (plant in a transparent polythene bag), the learners concluded that:
fields.explanation: When a plant is covered with a polythene bag, water droplets appear on the bag showing that leaves give off water vapor through transpiration

fields.question_text: Which characteristic of plants is demonstrated in the picture below?
fields.explanation: The picture likely shows plants at different stages of development, demonstrating growth

fields.question_text: Three of the following are characteristics of all mammals except one. Which one is it?
fields.option_b: Body covered with hair or fur
fields.explanation: Not all mammals give birth to live young. Monotremes like platypus and echidna lay eggs

fields.question_text: Which of the following characteristics is true about reptiles and amphibians? They have __________
fields.explanation: Both reptiles and amphibians are cold-blooded animals with varying body temperatures that change with the environment

fields.question_text: Which of the following animals can be grouped with the animal drawn below?
fields.explanation: The animal shown is likely a reptile, and gecko, snake, and chameleon are all reptiles

fields.question_text: Which of the animals listed below has mammary glands, body covered with hair or fur?
fields.explanation: Bat is a mammal and has mammary glands and body covered with hair or fur. The others are birds or fish

fields.question_text: What property of liquids were they investigating?
fields.explanation: Liquids take the shape of their container, showing they have no definite shape

fields.question_text: Which one of the following is the best way to manage solid waste (iron sheets, nails, wire)?
fields.explanation: Metal waste like iron sheets, nails, and wire are best managed through recycling to create new metal products

fields.question_text: Which of the following statements is true about gases?
fields.explanation: Gases have definite mass but no definite shape or volume, and they do occupy space

fields.question_text: When modeling the respiratory system, the balloons represent __________

fields.question_text: Paul kicked a ball up in the air and realized that it fell down. Which type of force made it fall down?

fields.question_text: A resource person was talking about indigenous knowledge systems in African societies. The resource person highlighted the following types of indigenous knowledge systems except:
fields.explanation: While Africans had 'informal' education (proverbs/stories), the term 'Education and Technology' in this context refers to formal Western schooling and digital tech, which are imported systems. Medicine includes using neem (mwarobaini) or aloe vera for healing. Cultural covers rites of passage and community laws. Environmental conservation includes 'Sacred Grooves' (Kayas) where cutting trees was forbidden.

fields.question_text: The population of Germany and Kenya are similar in that both countries:
fields.explanation: In both countries, population is concentrated in certain areas such as cities, fertile lands, and industrial regions. Germany has a very low birth rate (aging population), while Kenya's life expectancy is significantly lower than Germany's. Kenya has a 'Youth' dependency (too many kids); Germany has an 'Aged' dependency (too many retirees).

fields.question_text: Grade 8 learners were given a description of a certain climatic region in Africa: (i) Experiences high temperatures (ii) Has a small difference between the highest and lowest temperature (iii) Receives heavy conventional rainfall (iv) Has high humidity throughout the year. Identify the climatic region described above.
fields.explanation: Equatorial climate is characterized by high temperatures, heavy rainfall throughout the year, small temperature range, and high humidity. Mediterranean climate has hot dry summers and cool wet winters. Mountain climate has temperatures that vary with altitude and are generally cooler. Tropical climate is too general and does not specifically describe constant rainfall and humidity throughout the year.

fields.question_text: In grade 7, learners discussed values enshrined in Kenya's constitution. Which of the following is not one of those values?
fields.explanation: The constitution promotes fairness and justice, not unfairness. Human dignity promotes respect for all individuals. Patriotism and national unity are core national values that promote cohesion. Transparency and accountability are key principles required in governance and leadership.

fields.question_text: Early visitors to the continent of Africa played a crucial role in the partition and scramble of Africa. Who among the following early visitors was not a missionary?
fields.explanation: Henry Morton Stanley was mainly an explorer and journalist, not a missionary. Dr. Ludwig Krapf was a missionary who spread Christianity in East Africa. Johann Rebmann was also a missionary known for his work in East Africa. Dr. David Livingstone was both a missionary and an explorer who promoted Christianity and fought slave trade.

fields.question_text: Mzee Juma relocated from Nairobi city to his rural area after retirement from civil service. This type of migration is known as:
fields.explanation: Urban – rural migration is correct because the movement is from Nairobi (urban) to a rural area. Rural – rural migration refers to movement from one rural area to another. Rural – urban migration refers to movement from village to city. Urban – urban migration refers to movement between cities.

fields.question_text: Which one of the following is the best way to solve conflicts in the family?
fields.explanation: Dialogue allows open communication and understanding between family members. Mediation involves a third party and is not always necessary for family conflicts. Litigation involves courts and is too formal and costly for family issues. Arbitration involves an outsider making decisions, which may not promote mutual understanding.

fields.question_text: Walter has interest in becoming an astronaut. Which pathway should he select in senior school for him to pursue his career in future?
fields.explanation: Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM) provides knowledge in physics, engineering, and space science required for astronauts. Arts and sports science does not provide the scientific foundation needed for space careers. Social sciences focuses on human society and does not prepare one for astronaut training. Computer studies alone is limited and does not cover all the scientific areas required.

fields.question_text: Which European country colonized Mozambique during the scramble and partition of Africa?
fields.explanation: Portugal colonized Mozambique and Angola. Belgium colonized Congo, not Mozambique. France colonized many West African territories. Britain colonized countries such as Kenya, Uganda, and Nigeria.

fields.question_text: The time in Accra is 9:00 a.m. What will be the time in Dakar Senegal 17°W?
fields.explanation: Dakar is west of Accra, meaning it is behind in time. The Earth rotates 360° in 24 hours, so 15° = 1 hour and 1° = 4 minutes. Difference = 17° × 4 minutes = 68 minutes (1 hour 8 minutes). 9:00 a.m minus 1 hour 8 minutes = 7:52 a.m.

fields.question_text: What is the next number in the pattern? 8, 24, 72, 216, _______
