fields.option_a: The main function of a wholesaler is to buy goods in large quantities from producers and sell them in smaller quantities to retailers.

fields.question_text: How does a wholesaler differ from a retailer?
fields.explanation:
Wholesaler sells to retailers, while retailer sells to final consumers.
Wholesaler deals in larger quantities, while retailer deals in smaller quantities.
Wholesaler operates in warehouses, while retailer operates in shops.

fields.question_text: Give reasons why a producer may prefer to use agents instead of selling directly to wholesalers or retailers.
fields.explanation:
Agents do not take title, so the producer retains ownership and control.
Agents have specialized knowledge of particular markets.
It is cheaper than maintaining own sales force.
Agents can reach distant or foreign markets easily.
Suitable when the producer wants to sell on credit but does not want to take the risk

fields.question_text: Which of the following is the main difference between embedded computers and general purpose computers?
fields.option_c: Embedded computers are larger in size
fields.option_d: Embedded computers need constant human control
fields.explanation: This is the main distinction: Embedded computers are single-purpose, while general purpose computers are multi-purpose.

fields.question_text: Why do modern household appliances mostly use embedded computers?
fields.option_a: They are cheap and consume very little power
fields.option_b: They can be used to play games and watch movies
fields.option_c: They require a keyboard and mouse to operate
fields.option_d: They are very large and powerful
fields.explanation: Embedded computers are small, cheap to produce, use very little electricity, and are designed for one specific function — making them ideal for household appliances.

fields.question_text: Which of the following statements is true about general purpose computers?
fields.option_a: They perform only one specific task
fields.option_b: They are built inside household appliances
fields.option_d: They are mostly found in cars and microwaves
fields.explanation: General purpose computers are flexible and can run different programs to perform tasks such as typing, calculations, internet browsing, and gaming.

fields.question_text: One characteristic of embedded computers is that they
fields.explanation: Embedded computers are small and are integrated (built) inside other machines and appliances to control their operations

fields.question_text: An ATM machine is an example of a
fields.explanation: An ATM is designed for one specific purpose — cash withdrawal and banking services. It is therefore a dedicated/special purpose computer.

fields.question_text: Dedicated computers are also referred to as
fields.explanation: Dedicated computers (special purpose computers) are designed to perform only one specific task. They cannot be used for other tasks.

fields.question_text: Which one of the following is NOT an embedded computer?
fields.explanation:
A smartphone is a general-purpose computer because it can perform many different tasks — making calls, browsing the internet, playing games, taking photos, running different apps, etc.
Embedded computers are designed to perform only one specific task and are built into a device (e.g. washing machine, microwave, car engine).

fields.question_text: Which of the following is the best example of an embedded computer?
fields.explanation: A microwave oven has a small computer built inside it to control time, temperature, and power levels. This is a typical example of an embedded computer.

fields.question_text: Teacher asked Grade 9 South learners to discuss types of computers that are integrated in machines and household appliances. These types of computers are referred to as
fields.explanation: Embedded computers are small computers built inside other machines and household appliances to perform specific tasks. They are hidden and work automatically (e.g. in washing machines, microwaves, cars).

fields.question_text: Which type of computer works with continuous data such as temperature and speed?
fields.explanation: Analogue computers process continuous (analogue) data. They measure physical quantities like temperature, pressure, and speed.

fields.question_text: A computer that can process both continuous and discrete data is called a
fields.explanation: Hybrid computers combine the features of both analogue and digital computers. They are used in places where both types of data need to be processed.

fields.question_text: Which of the following is the best example of a digital computer?
fields.explanation: Laptops work using discrete data (0s and 1s) and are therefore digital computers.

fields.question_text: Hybrid computers are mostly used in
fields.explanation: Hybrid computers are used where high speed (from analogue) and high accuracy (from digital) are both required, such as in hospitals and scientific research.

fields.question_text: Embedded computers are mostly classified under
fields.explanation:
Explanation:
Embedded computers are classified according to purpose because they are built into devices to perform specific tasks.

fields.question_text: Which of the following is not a digital computer?
fields.explanation: A petrol pump meter is usually an analogue or hybrid device because it measures continuous quantities (volume of fuel).

fields.question_text: Describe the three types of computers classified according to functionality. Give one example for each type.
fields.explanation:
Digital Computers: These process data in the form of discrete numbers (0s and 1s). They give very accurate results.
Example: Laptop, Desktop, Calculator.
Analogue Computers: These process continuous data such as temperature, speed, pressure, or voltage. They are fast but less accurate.
Example: Speedometer, Thermometer, Old aircraft simulators.
Hybrid Computers: These combine the features of both digital and analogue computers. They can process both continuous and discrete data.
Example: ECG machine in hospitals, Petrol station pumps, Weather forecasting systems.

fields.question_text:
Digital Computers: These process data in the form of discrete numbers (0s and 1s). They give very accurate results.
Example: Laptop, Desktop, Calculator.
Analogue Computers: These process continuous data such as temperature, speed, pressure, or voltage. They are fast but less accurate.
Example: Speedometer, Thermometer, Old aircraft simulators.
Hybrid Computers: These combine the features of both digital and analogue computers. They can process both continuous and discrete data.
Example: ECG machine in hospitals, Petrol station pumps, Weather forecasting systems.
fields.explanation:
General Purpose Computers: These are versatile computers that can perform many different tasks depending on the software installed. They are flexible and can be used for various purposes.
Examples: Laptop, Desktop computer, Smartphone, Tablet.
Special Purpose Computers (Dedicated computers): These are designed to perform only one specific task. They cannot be used for other tasks.
Examples: ATM machine, Traffic light controller, Digital thermometer, Washing machine controller.

fields.question_text: Explain three ways in which computers can be classified.
fields.explanation:
According to Size – Based on their physical size and processing power (Supercomputers, Mainframe, Minicomputers, Microcomputers).
According to Purpose – Based on the tasks they perform (General purpose and Special purpose/Dedicated computers).
According to Functionality – Based on how they process data (Digital, Analogue, and Hybrid computers).

fields.question_text: Computers can be classified according to size. List the four types in order from the largest to the smallest.
fields.explanation:
Supercomputers – Largest and most powerful computers. Used for complex calculations (e.g. weather forecasting, space research).
Mainframe computers – Very large and expensive. Used by big organizations like banks and government for handling huge amounts of data.
Minicomputers – Medium-sized. Less powerful than mainframes but more powerful than microcomputers.
Microcomputers – Smallest computers. Also called personal computers.
Examples: Desktops, Laptops, Tablets.

fields.question_text:
Identify the type of computer described below:
It is the largest, most powerful and most expensive computer in the world. 
It can perform trillions of calculations per second and is used for complex scientific research, weather forecasting and space exploration.
fields.explanation: This is a Supercomputer. It is the largest and most powerful type of computer. It is specially designed to process extremely large amounts of data at very high speeds. It is used for complex tasks such as weather prediction, scientific simulations, and space research.

fields.question_text:
Identify the type of computer described below:
This is a very large and expensive computer. It is used by big organizations such as banks, insurance companies and government departments to process and store huge amounts of data. It can serve many users at the same time.
fields.explanation: This is a Mainframe computer. Mainframes are very large, powerful, and expensive computers. They are used by large organizations to handle and process huge volumes of data and support many users simultaneously.

fields.question_text:
Identify the type of computer described below:
It is a medium-sized computer. It is more powerful than a microcomputer but less powerful than a mainframe computer. It is mostly used by medium-sized organizations and research institutions.
fields.explanation:
Explanation:
This is a Minicomputer. It is a medium-sized computer in terms of size and power. It is more powerful than microcomputers but less powerful than mainframe computers. It is commonly used by small to medium-sized organizations.

fields.question_text:
Identify the type of computer described below:
These are the smallest and cheapest types of computers. They are also called personal computers. They include desktops, laptops, tablets and smartphones.
fields.explanation: This is a Microcomputer. Microcomputers are the smallest computers in the classification according to size. They are also known as personal computers (PCs). They are affordable and widely used by individuals. Examples include desktops, laptops, tablets, and smartphones.

fields.question_text: It is used for very complex calculations such as simulating nuclear explosions, climate modeling and scientific research that requires massive processing power.”
fields.explanation: Supercomputers are the fastest and most powerful computers available. They are used for highly complex and demanding tasks that require massive processing power and speed, such as scientific research, nuclear testing simulations, and advanced weather forecasting.

fields.question_text: According to CBE, one of the main goals of Junior Secondary is to help learners
fields.option_a: memorize facts for continuous assessments and examinations
fields.option_b: explore and nurture their talents and interests
fields.option_c: focus only on core academic subjects

fields.question_text: Sarah loves drama and acting. She always participates actively in school plays. To nurture her talent, the school should
fields.option_a: tell her to focus on STEM subjects
fields.option_b: assign her a drama coach to train her
fields.option_c: encourage her to become the class monitor
fields.option_d: register her for spelling bee competitions

fields.question_text: A mason is marking out the position for a slanted drainage channel that needs to be laid at a specific angle of 45 degrees for proper water flow. Which one of the following tools would be most suitable for this work?
fields.explanation: A protractor is used to measure, mark, or verify specific angles other than 90 degrees (such as 45°, 30°, 60°, etc.). It is suitable when an exact non-right angle is required, like in drainage slopes or angled brickwork. Try square only checks 90° angles, while the others do not measure angles.

fields.question_text: During a metalwork project, a learner has measured the position for holes on a mild steel plate. He now needs to clearly indicate the exact centre points so they do not shift during drilling. Which tool should he use?
fields.explanation: A dot punch (or centre punch) is a marking tool used to create a small indentation at measured points. This prevents the drill bit from wandering and ensures accuracy when starting holes in metal.

fields.question_text: A learner is working on a sheet metal project and needs to cut a straight line through thin galvanized iron without bending the edges. Which tool is most appropriate?
fields.explanation: Tin snips (or sheet metal snips) are cutting tools designed specifically for cutting thin sheets of metal cleanly and accurately, similar to scissors but stronger for metal work.

fields.question_text: State the name of the tool show below and its use
State the use of the cutting tool shown below
fields.explanation: A tenon saw (a type of back saw) is used for making precise, straight cuts in wood, typically for joinery. The heavy brass or steel "back" keeps the blade stiff, preventing it from bending during the stroke.

fields.question_text:
Study the diagram of the saw shown below.
The tool in the diagram is least suitable for:
fields.option_d: Making accurate cross cuts in small wood pieces
fields.explanation: The tenon saw is designed for straight cuts only. For curves, a coping saw or fret saw would be used instead.

fields.question_text:
Jack is learning about visual programming applications. What are the key features of visual
programming applications to enhance the effectiveness and user experience of a visual programming
application?
In visual programming, blocks that allow the program to receive information from the user (such as pressing a key or clicking the mouse) represent:
A learner creates a program where a sprite moves across the screen and plays a sound when it touches an object. The movement and sound represent
Why is drag and drop an important feature in visual programming applications?
Visual programming applications use blocks with specific shapes that only "snap" together if the logic is correct. This feature is designed to prevent which type of error?
fields.explanation: In text-based coding, a missing comma or bracket causes a syntax error; in visual programming, the puzzle-piece shapes (syntax constraints) physically prevent the user from connecting incompatible commands, thereby eliminating the possibility of a "grammar" mistake in the code.

fields.question_text: Jack wants his sprite to move 10 steps forward and repeat this action 5 times without him having to drag the same block five times. Which category of blocks should he use to find a "Repeat" command?
fields.explanation: Control blocks manage the "flow" of a program, including loops (repeat, forever) and conditional statements (if-then). Motion blocks only handle movement, Sensing handles inputs like mouse clicks, and Operators handle mathematical calculations.

fields.question_text: In visual programming applications, the blocks are color-coded to help learners. If a student is looking for blocks related to addition, subtraction, or "greater than" comparisons, they should look for the color usually associated with
fields.explanation: Operators are the blocks used for mathematical and logical comparisons (usually green in applications like Scratch). Variables handle data storage, Events handle starting the program (like clicking the green flag), and Sound handles audio output.

fields.question_text: In the context of visual programming for Junior Secondary School, what is a "Sprite"?
fields.explanation: an object or character that can be controlled by code. Sprites are the central actors in a visual program that follow the instructions given in the scripts. They are not variables (data), music (sound), or the hardware (screen) itself.

fields.question_text:
A learner is programming a "Smart Fan" that only turns on if the room gets too hot.
State the Selection Statement being used in this automation logic.

A student is creating a "Space Shooter" game. They use the following code to manage the player's status.
Identify the variable used in this game logic.

A student wants to draw a "Spiral" pattern on the stage.
Which category of blocks is responsible for the "Repeat [100]" instruction?
fields.explanation: The Control category contains all "Looping" or "Repeating" blocks. These blocks control the flow of the program by determining how many times a sequence of actions should be performed.

fields.question_text: A boundary dispute arises between two neighbours over a fence line. They invite a respected village elder to listen to both sides and make a final decision that both must follow. This process is called
fields.explanation: In arbitration, an independent third party (arbitrator) hears both sides and gives a binding (enforceable) decision. It is faster and less expensive than going to court (litigation).

fields.question_text: Two neighbouring communities in a dry area are fighting over access to a shared water point. They decide to sit together and discuss how to share the water fairly without involving any outsider. Which method of conflict resolution are they using?
fields.explanation: Negotiation is when the parties in conflict talk directly to each other and reach an agreement voluntarily. It is cheap, fast, and promotes ownership of the solution, which helps in maintaining long-term peaceful relations.

fields.question_text: Define the term conflict resolution.
fields.explanation: Conflict resolution is the process of ending a disagreement or dispute between two or more parties in a peaceful manner.

fields.question_text: During a Social Studies lesson, learners were asked to explain how good neighbourliness can reduce conflicts in the community.
In a football match, a team scored 4 goals, conceded 6 goals, then scored 3 more goals. What is their goal difference?
A number is decreased by 8, increased by 15, then decreased by 6. What is the overall change?
A hiker climbed 12 m up a hill, slipped down 7 m, then climbed another 4 m. What is his total change in position?
A football team lost 3 points for a foul, gained 7 points in the next match, and lost 2 points later. What is the net score?
A lift started at the ground floor. It went down 4 floors, up 9 floors, then down 2 floors. Where did it end?
A bank account was overdrawn by KSh 2,500. A deposit of KSh 4,000 was made, followed by a withdrawal of KSh 1,200. What is the new balance?
fields.explanation:
Overdrawn means the balance is negative: −2,500
Deposit: −2500 + 4000 = 1500
Withdrawal: 1500 − 1200 = 300

fields.question_text: Which of the following are integers?
fields.explanation:
Integers are whole numbers and their negatives, including zero.
Fractions and decimals are not integers.

fields.question_text:
Arrange the following integers in ascending order
−3, 5, 0, −1
fields.explanation:
Ascending order means smallest to largest.
Negative numbers come before zero, and zero comes before positive numbers.

fields.question_text:
Arrange the integers in descending order:
2, −6, 0, 4
fields.explanation:
Descending order means largest to smallest.
Positive numbers come first, then zero, then negative numbers.

fields.question_text: John used $\frac{1}{5}$ of the fertilizer on tomatoes. He used $\frac{1}{2}$ of the remainder on cabbagesand the rest was used on maize. What fraction of the fertilizer was used on maize?
fields.explanation: Determine the amount used on tomatoes:John used $\frac{1}{5}$ of the fertilizer on tomatoes.Calculate the remainder:To find the remainder, subtract the amount used on tomatoes from the whole ($1$):$$1 - \frac{1}{5} = \frac{4}{5}$$So, $\frac{4}{5}$ of the fertilizer remains.Determine the amount used on cabbages:He used $\frac{1}{2}$ of the remainder on cabbages:$$\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{4}{5} = \frac{4}{10} = \frac{2}{5}$$Calculate the fraction used on maize:The rest was used on maize. We subtract the portions for tomatoes and cabbages from the total, or simply subtract the cabbage portion from the previous remainder:$$\frac{4}{5} (\text{remainder}) - \frac{2}{5} (\text{cabbages}) = \frac{2}{5}$$Conclusion:The fraction of fertilizer used on maize was $\frac{2}{5}$.

fields.question_text: An engineer measured the length of a piece of cable as $0.\dot{3}\dot{6}$ Convert the length into a fraction.
fields.explanation: Assign the decimal to a variableLet $x$ represent the recurring decimal:$$x = 0.363636...$$Step 2: Multiply to shift the decimal pointSince there are two repeating digits ($3$ and $6$), multiply both sides by $10^2$ (which is $100$):$$100x = 36.363636...$$Step 3: Subtract the original equationNow, subtract the first equation from the second to eliminate the repeating part:$$(100x = 36.363636...)$$$$- (x = 0.363636...)$$$$99x = 36$$Step 4: Solve for $x$Divide both sides by $99$:$$x = \frac{36}{99}$$Step 5: Simplify the fractionBoth $36$ and $99$ are divisible by $9$:$$\frac{36 \div 9}{99 \div 9} = \frac{4}{11}$$Final Answer:The length of the cable as a fraction is $\frac{4}{11}$.

fields.question_text: A metal rod has a diameter of $0.8\dot{3}$ cm. Convert this into a fraction in its simplest form.
fields.explanation: Step 1: Assign the variableLet $x = 0.8333...$Step 2: Eliminate the non-repeating partMultiply by 10 to move the decimal to the repeating part:$10x = 8.333...$ (Equation 1)Step 3: Create a second equationMultiply Equation 1 by 10 (since 1 digit repeats):$100x = 83.333...$ (Equation 2)Step 4: Subtract$$(100x = 83.333...)$$$$- (10x = 8.333...)$$$90x = 75$Step 5: Simplify$x = \frac{75}{90}$Divide both by 15:$x = \frac{5}{6}$

fields.question_text: Express $0.\dot{1}2\dot{3}$ as a fraction.
fields.explanation: Problem: Express $0.\dot{1}2\dot{3}$ as a fraction.Step 1: Assign the variableLet $x = 0.123123...$Step 2: Multiply to shift the repeating blockSince three digits repeat ($123$), multiply by $1,000$:$1000x = 123.123123...$Step 3: Subtract$$(1000x = 123.123123...)$$$$- (x = 0.123123...)$$$999x = 123$Step 4: Simplify$x = \frac{123}{999}$Divide both by 3:$x = \frac{41}{333}$

fields.question_text: Convert $0.0\dot{4}\dot{5}$ to a fraction.
fields.explanation: Step 1: Assign the variableLet $x = 0.0454545...$Step 2: Shift to the start of the repeatMultiply by 10:$10x = 0.454545...$ (Equation 1)Step 3: Shift past the repeating blockMultiply Equation 1 by 100:$1000x = 45.454545...$ (Equation 2)Step 4: Subtract$1000x - 10x = 45$$990x = 45$Step 5: Simplify$x = \frac{45}{990}$Divide both by 45:$x = \frac{1}{22}$

fields.question_text: Convert $3.\dot{7}\dot{2}$ to a fraction. (leave your answer as improper)
Convert $1.0\dot{5}$ to an improper fraction
What is $0.12\dot{7}$ as a fraction
A square-shaped volleyball court has an area of $163.8 \text{ m}^2$. A referee walked round it once. What distance did he cover? (Use mathematical tables)
The perimeter of a square floor is $25.32 \text{ m}$. Using mathematical tables, find the area of the floor.
The area of a square garden is $73.5 \text{ m}^2$. The owner wants to fence it with three strands of wire. Find the total length of wire required. (use mathematical tables)
fields.explanation: Step 1: Find the side ($s$) using Square Root Tables ($10 - 100$)Row $73$, Column $5$ $\rightarrow$ $8.573$.Side length $= \mathbf{8.573 \text{ m}}$.Step 2: Find the PerimeterPerimeter $= 8.573 \times 4 = \mathbf{34.292 \text{ m}}$.Step 3: Calculate total wireTotal wire $= 34.292 \times 3 \text{ strands} = \mathbf{102.876 \text{ m}}$.

fields.question_text: Given that the area of a square is $0.0542 \text{ m}^2$, find the length of its side.
fields.explanation: Step 1: Find the square root using tables$\sqrt{0.0542} = \sqrt{5.42 \times 10^{-2}}$.Look up $\sqrt{5.42}$ in the $1.0 - 10$ table.Row $5.4$, Column $2$ $\rightarrow$ $2.328$.Adjust for $10^{-2}$ ($\times 10^{-1}$): $0.2328 \text{ m}$.

used was $\frac{2}{3}$
of the mixture. Determine the ratio of sand to cement in the mixture

fields.question_text: A painter mixed yellow and blue paint to get a green shade. If $\frac{3}{5}$ of the mixture is yellow paint, find the ratio of blue paint to yellow paint.
fields.explanation:
Step 1: Find the fraction of blue paintRemainder = $1 - \frac{3}{5} = \frac{2}{5}$Step 2: Express as a ratioYellow : Blue = $\frac{3}{5} : \frac{2}{5}$Step 3: Simplify Multiply both sides by 5:Ratio = $3 : 2$
But question asks for blue paint : yellow paint
So 2:3

fields.question_text: A mechanic has a metal alloy made of copper and zinc. Zinc makes up $40\%$ of the alloy. Determine the ratio of copper to zinc in its simplest form.
fields.explanation: Step 1: Find the percentage of copperCopper = $100\% - 40\% = 60\%$Step 2: Express as a ratioCopper : Zinc = $60 : 40$Step 3: SimplifyDivide by 20:Ratio = $3 : 2$

fields.question_text: A chef makes a fruit punch where $\frac{2}{7}$ of the mixture is concentrated juice and the rest is water. Find the ratio of water to concentrated juice.
fields.explanation: Step 1: Find the fraction of waterWater = $1 - \frac{2}{7} = \frac{5}{7}$Step 2: Express as a ratio (Water to Juice)Water : Juice = $\frac{5}{7} : \frac{2}{7}$Step 3: SimplifyRatio = $5 : 2$

fields.question_text: In a certain Junior School, $\frac{4}{9}$ of the students are boys. Find the ratio of boys to girls.
fields.explanation: Step 1: Find the fraction of girlsGirls = $1 - \frac{4}{9} = \frac{5}{9}$Step 2: Express as a ratioBoys : Girls = $\frac{4}{9} : \frac{5}{9}$Step 3: SimplifyRatio = $4 : 5$

fields.question_text: Three partners Musa, Jane, and Otieno shared a profit of Sh. 48,000 in the ratio $3 : 2 : 5$ respectively. How much did Otieno receive?
fields.explanation: Step-by-Step Solution:Total Parts: $3 + 2 + 5 = 10$ parts.Value of 1 Part: $48,000 \div 10 = \text{Sh. } 4,800$.Otieno's Share (5 parts): $4,800 \times 5 = \mathbf{\text{Sh. } 24,000}$.

fields.question_text: The perimeter of a triangle is $72 \text{ cm}$. The sides are in the ratio $3 : 4 : 5$. Find the length of the longest side.
fields.explanation: Step-by-Step Solution:Total Parts: $3 + 4 + 5 = 12$ parts.Value of 1 Part: $72 \div 12 = 6 \text{ cm}$.Longest Side (5 parts): $6 \times 5 = \mathbf{30 \text{ cm}}$.

fields.question_text: A piece of land measuring $15.6 \text{ hectares}$ is shared between a school and a church in the ratio $7 : 5$. How much more land did the school get than the church?
fields.explanation:
Step-by-Step Solution:
Total Parts: $7 + 5 = 12$ parts.Value of 1 Part: $15.6 \div 12 = 1.3 \text{ hectares}$.Difference in Shares: $7 - 5 = 2$ parts.Final Calculation: $1.3 \times 2 = \mathbf{2.6 \text{ hectares}}$.

fields.question_text: A farmer mixes two types of feed, A and B, in the ratio $2 : 3$. If the total mass of the mixture is $250 \text{ kg}$, find the mass of feed Type B in the mixture.
fields.explanation:
Step-by-Step Solution:
Total Parts: $2 + 3 = 5$ parts.Value of 1 Part: $250 \div 5 = 50 \text{ kg}$.Type B (3 parts): $50 \times 3 = \mathbf{150 \text{ kg}}$.

fields.question_text: A bus company increased its fare by 15%. If the new fare is Sh. 460, what was the fare before the increase?
fields.explanation: Step-by-Step Solution:Identify the percentage: $100\% + 15\% = 115\%$.Set up the equation: $115\% = 460$.Solve for 100%:$\frac{460}{115} \times 100$Calculate: $4 \times 100 = \mathbf{\text{Sh. } 400}$.

fields.question_text: Following a fuel subsidy, a transport Sacco decreased its fare by 25%. If the original fare was Sh. 200, find the new fare.
fields.explanation: Step-by-Step Solution:Find the remaining percentage: $100\% - 25\% = 75\%$.Calculate new fare:$\frac{75}{100} \times 200$Simplify: $75 \times 2 = \mathbf{\text{Sh. } 150}$.

fields.question_text: The fare from Town A to Town B was Sh. 150. During the festive season, the fare increased by 20%. After the season, the new fare was decreased by 10%. Calculate the final fare.
fields.explanation: Step-by-Step Solution:Calculate the first increase (20%):New fare $= 120\% \text{ of } 150$$\frac{120}{100} \times 150 = \text{Sh. } 180$Calculate the subsequent decrease (10%):The decrease applies to the NEW fare (Sh. 180).Final fare $= 90\% \text{ of } 180$$\frac{90}{100} \times 180 = \mathbf{\text{Sh. } 162}$

fields.question_text: A shopkeeper increased the price of a shirt by 25%. During a sale, he decreased the new price by 25%. The final selling price was Sh. 1,125. Calculate the original price of the shirt.
fields.explanation: Step 1: Find price before 25% decrease$$75\% = 1,125$$$$100\% = \frac{1,125}{75} \times 100 = \mathbf{\text{Sh. } 1,500}$$Step 2: Find original price before 25% increase$$125\% = 1,500$$$$100\% = \frac{1,500}{125} \times 100 = \mathbf{\text{Sh. } 1,200}$$

fields.question_text: The value of a piece of land was Sh. 500,000. The value appreciated (increased) by 15% in the first year and appreciated by 10% in the second year. What was the value of the land at the end of the second year?
fields.explanation: Step-by-Step Solution:End of Year 1:$115\% \text{ of } 500,000 = 1.15 \times 500,000 = \text{Sh. } 575,000$End of Year 2:$110\% \text{ of } 575,000 = 1.1 \times 575,000 = \mathbf{\text{Sh. } 632,500}$

fields.question_text: A motorcycle valued at Sh. 120,000 depreciates at a rate of 10% per year. Calculate its value after two years.
fields.explanation: Step-by-Step Solution:Value after Year 1:$90\% \text{ of } 120,000 = 0.9 \times 120,000 = \text{Sh. } 108,000$Value after Year 2:$90\% \text{ of } 108,000 = 0.9 \times 108,000 = \mathbf{\text{Sh. } 97,200}$

fields.question_text: A salesman's monthly salary was increased by 10% in the first year and then later increased by another 10% in the second year. His salary became Sh. 24,200. after the second increase. Calculate his original salary before the first increase.
fields.explanation: Step-by-Step Solution:Reverse the second increase (10%):$110\% = 24,200$$100\% = \frac{24,200}{110} \times 100 = \text{Sh. } 22,000$Reverse the first increase (10%):$110\% = 22,000$$100\% = \frac{22,000}{110} \times 100 = \mathbf{\text{Sh. } 20,000}$

fields.question_text: A retailer bought a bag of sugar for Sh. 8,000. He increased the price by 20% for his retail customers. Later, to clear old stock, he gave a 20% discount on the new price. Calculate the final selling price.
fields.explanation: Step-by-Step Solution:First increase (20%):$120\% \text{ of } 8,000 = \frac{120}{100} \times 8,000 = \text{Sh. } 9,600$Discount (20%):The discount is taken from Sh. 9,600.$80\% \text{ of } 9,600 = \frac{80}{100} \times 9,600 = \mathbf{\text{Sh. } 7,680}$

fields.question_text:
Given that: $$m = \frac{c^2 + 3d}{4}$$
and if $c = 8$ and $d = 4$, find m
fields.explanation: To find the value of $m$, substitute the values of $c$ and $d$ into the formula:$$m = \frac{c^2 + 3d}{4}$$Step 1: Substitute the valuesSubstitute $c = 8$ and $d = 4$:$$m = \frac{8^2 + 3(4)}{4}$$Step 2: Calculate the numeratorFirst, find the square of $c$:$$8^2 = 64$$Next, find the value of $3d$:$$3 \times 4 = 12$$Add them together:$$64 + 12 = 76$$Step 3: Divide by 4Now, divide the result by the denominator:$$m = \frac{76}{4}$$$$m = 19$$Final Answer:The value of $m$ is $19$.

fields.question_text: Evaluate the expression $\frac{x^2 - y}{2}$ when $x = -4$ and $y = 6$.
fields.explanation: Step-by-Step Solution:Substitute values: $\frac{(-4)^2 - 6}{2}$Square the negative number: $(-4) \times (-4) = 16$ (Crucial: result is positive)Subtract $y$: $16 - 6 = 10$Divide: $10 \div 2 = \mathbf{5}$

fields.question_text: Find the value of $y$ in  $y = a(b - c)^2$ when $a = 2$, $b = 7$, and $c = 4$.
fields.explanation: Step-by-Step Solution:Substitute values: $y = 2(7 - 4)^2$Solve inside brackets: $7 - 4 = 3$Square the result: $3^2 = 9$Multiply by $a$: $2 \times 9 = \mathbf{18}$

fields.question_text: Given that $p = 5$ and $q = p -2$, evaluate the expression:$$3p - 2q + 7$$
If $u = 8$ and $v = 2u - 10$, evaluate:$$\frac{v^2}{u + 4}$$
fields.explanation: Step-by-Step Solution:$v = 16 - 10 = 6$$\frac{6^2}{8 + 4} = \frac{36}{12} = \mathbf{3}$

fields.question_text:
Lucy bought 4 exercise books and 8 pens for sh 600. Joy bought 2 similar exercise books and 6
similar pens for sh 350 from the same shop. Calculate the cost of a pen.
fields.explanation: Let the cost of an exercise book be $x$ and the cost of a pen be $y$.Lucy’s purchase: $4x + 8y = 600$  --- (Equation 1)Joy’s purchase: $2x + 6y = 350$  --- (Equation 2)Step 2: Use the Elimination MethodTo find the cost of a pen ($y$), we should eliminate $x$. We can do this by making the coefficient of $x$ in Equation 2 equal to the coefficient of $x$ in Equation 1.Multiply Equation 2 by 2:$$2(2x + 6y = 350) \Rightarrow 4x + 12y = 700$$--- (Equation 3)Step 3: Subtract Equation 1 from Equation 3$$(4x + 12y) - (4x + 8y) = 700 - 600$$This simplifies to:$$4y = 100$$Step 4: Solve for $y$$$y = \frac{100}{4}$$$$y = 25$$Final Answer:The cost of a pen is Sh. 25.

fields.question_text: A bus company charges a boarding fee plus Sh 15 per kilometer. A 6 km trip costs Sh 105. A 10 km trip costs Sh 165. Find the boarding fee and cost per km.
fields.explanation:
Equations:
x + 6y = 105
x + 10y = 165

Solve: y = 15, x = 15

Answer: Boarding fee = Sh 15, per km = Sh 15

fields.question_text: Lucy bought 4 notebooks and 8 pens for Sh 600. Joy bought 2 notebooks and 6 pens for Sh 350. How much more did Lucy spend on pens than Joy?
fields.explanation:
Let price of notebook = x, price of pen = y

Equations:
4x + 8y = 600
2x + 6y = 350

Use elimination: multiply second by 2 → 4x + 12y = 700
Subtract first equation: (4x + 12y) - (4x + 8y) = 700 - 600 → 4y = 100 → y = 25

Lucy spent on pens: 8 × 25 = 200
Joy spent on pens: 6 × 25 = 150

Answer: Sh 50 more

fields.question_text: A shop sold 3 pens and 2 pencils for Sh 70. Another sale: 5 pens and 3 pencils for Sh 120. If a customer buys 2 pens and 3 pencils, how much will he pay in total?
fields.explanation:
Solution:
3x + 2y = 70
5x + 3y = 120

Solve: x = 20, y = 5

Total cost: 2×20 + 3×5 = 40 + 15 = 55

Answer: Sh 55

fields.question_text: Aisha bought 3 notebooks and 5 pens for Sh 460. Ben bought 2 notebooks and 3 pens for Sh 280. How much more did Ben spend on notebooks than on pens?
fields.explanation:
Solution:
x = 60, y = 40

Ben spent on notebooks: 2 × 60 = 120
Ben spent on pens: 3 × 40 = 120

Difference = 0

Answer: Sh 0 (equal)

fields.question_text: Lucy bought 4 exercise books and 8 pens for Sh 600. Joy bought 2 exercise books and 6 pens for Sh 350. What is the ratio of money Lucy spent on pens to Joy spent on exercise books?
fields.explanation:
Solution:
y = 25, x = 100

Lucy pens: 8 × 25 = 200
Joy exercise books: 2 × 100 = 200

Ratio = 200:200 = 1:1

Answer: 1:1

fields.question_text: The minute hand of a wall clock is 14 cm long. It moved through an angle of 30°. Calculate the distance covered by the tip of the minute hand. Round to one decimal place

A heavy-duty pendulum in a museum is **1.5 metres** long. During a single swing from one extreme to the other, it covers a horizontal arc through an angle of **40°**.
Calculate the distance (arc length) traveled by the tip of the pendulum in one swing.
(Give your answer in metres, rounded to 2 decimal places. Take $\pi = \frac{22}{7}$)
fields.explanation:
To find the distance traveled (arc length) by the tip of the pendulum, we use the formula for the length of an arc:

$$\text{Arc length} = \frac{\theta}{360} \times 2\pi r$$

### **Step 1: Identify the values**
* **Radius ($r$):** The length of the pendulum = **1.5 m**
* **Angle ($\theta$):** The angle of the swing = **40°**
* **Pi ($\pi$):** **$\frac{22}{7}$**

### **Step 2: Substitute into the formula**
$$\text{Arc length} = \frac{40}{360} \times 2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 1.5$$

### **Step 3: Simplify the calculation**
First, simplify the fraction:
$$\frac{40}{360} = \frac{1}{9}$$

Now, multiply the terms:
$$\text{Arc length} = \frac{1}{9} \times 2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 1.5$$
$$\text{Arc length} = \frac{1 \times 2 \times 22 \times 1.5}{9 \times 7}$$
$$\text{Arc length} = \frac{66}{63}$$

### **Step 4: Solve for the final value**
$$\text{Arc length} = 1.0476...$$

Rounding to **2 decimal places**, we get:
**1.05 metres**

fields.question_text:
A circular sprinkler at a farm in Mwea rotates through an angle of **120°**. If the water reaches a maximum distance of **14 m** from the sprinkler, calculate the **area** of the grass that is watered.

(Take $\pi = \frac{22}{7}$)
fields.explanation: Step 1: Identify the valuesRadius ($r$): 21 mAngle ($\theta$): 120°$\pi$: $\frac{22}{7}$Step 2: Substitute into the Area of a Sector formula$$\text{Area} = \frac{\theta}{360} \times \pi r^2$$$$\text{Area} = \frac{120}{360} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 21 \times 21$$Step 3: Simplify and CalculateSimplify the fraction: $\frac{120}{360} = \frac{1}{3}$Simplify the radius with the denominator: $21 \div 7 = 3$$$\text{Area} = \frac{1}{3} \times 22 \times 3 \times 21$$The 3 in the numerator and 3 in the denominator cancel out:$$\text{Area} = 22 \times 21$$Step 4: Final Calculation$$22 \times 21 = 462$$Final Answer:The area of the grass that is watered is 462 m²

fields.question_text:
A technician in Garissa has a solar tracking sensor. The sensor moves along a circular track with a radius of 21 cm. If the sensor travels a distance (arc length) of 22 cm to follow the sun, calculate the angle ($\theta$) through which it has rotated. 

(Take $\pi = \frac{22}{7}$)
fields.explanation:
To find the angle $\theta$ through which the sensor has rotated, we apply the relationship between arc length, radius, and the central angle.

### **1. Core Formula**
The distance traveled along a circular path (arc length) is calculated as:
$$s = r\theta$$
Where:
* $s = 22 \text{ cm}$ (Arc length)
* $r = 21 \text{ cm}$ (Radius)
* $\theta = \text{Angle in radians}$

### **2. Calculate Angle in Radians**
Rearrange the formula to solve for $\theta$:
$$\theta = \frac{s}{r}$$
$$\theta = \frac{22}{21} \text{ rad}$$

### **3. Convert to Degrees**
To convert from radians to degrees, multiply by $\frac{180}{\pi}$. Using $\pi = \frac{22}{7}$:
$$\text{Angle in degrees} = \frac{22}{21} \times \frac{180}{\pi}$$
$$\text{Angle in degrees} = \frac{22}{21} \times \frac{180}{\frac{22}{7}}$$

### **4. Final Simplification**
Invert the fraction and multiply:
$$\text{Angle in degrees} = \frac{22}{21} \times \frac{180 \times 7}{22}$$
* The **22s** cancel each other out.
* **7** goes into **21** three times ($21 \div 7 = 3$).
* The remaining values are $180 \div 3$.

**Result:**
$$\theta = 60^\circ$$

fields.question_text:
A group of students in a school in Eldoret designed a sector-shaped garden with a radius of 14 metres. If the garden covers a total area of 231 square metres, calculate the central angle ($\theta$) of this garden.

(Take $\pi = \frac{22}{7}$)
fields.explanation:
To calculate the central angle $\theta$ of the sector-shaped garden, we use the formula for the area of a sector.

### **1. Identify the Formula**
The area ($A$) of a sector is given by:
$$A = \frac{\theta}{360} \times \pi r^2$$
Where:
* $A = 231 \text{ m}^2$ (Area of the garden)
* $r = 14 \text{ m}$ (Radius)
* $\pi = \frac{22}{7}$



### **2. Set up the Equation**
Substitute the known values into the formula:
$$231 = \frac{\theta}{360} \times \frac{22}{7} \times (14 \times 14)$$

### **3. Simplify the Terms**
First, simplify the radius and $\pi$ part:
$$231 = \frac{\theta}{360} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 196$$
Since $196 \div 7 = 28$:
$$231 = \frac{\theta}{360} \times 22 \times 28$$
$$231 = \frac{\theta}{360} \times 616$$

### **4. Solve for $\theta$**
Isolate $\theta$ by multiplying both sides by 360 and dividing by 616:
$$\theta = \frac{231 \times 360}{616}$$

Simplify the fraction:
* Both 231 and 616 are divisible by 7 ($231 \div 7 = 33$ and $616 \div 7 = 88$).
$$\theta = \frac{33 \times 360}{88}$$
* Both 33 and 88 are divisible by 11 ($33 \div 11 = 3$ and $88 \div 11 = 8$).
$$\theta = \frac{3 \times 360}{8}$$
* $360 \div 8 = 45$.
$$\theta = 3 \times 45$$

**Final Answer:**
The central angle of the garden is **$135^\circ$**.

fields.question_text: Tom had a cylindrical flower vase with a radius of 28 cm and a height of 80 cm. He painted the outer part of the flower vase. Calculate the area of the surfaces painted.(Take $\pi = \frac{22}{7}$)
fields.explanation: . Identify the Surfaces to be PaintedA flower vase is a cylinder that is open at the top. Therefore, the painted area consists of:The curved surface area (the sides).The base (the bottom).2. State the FormulasCurved Surface Area ($CSA$) $= 2\pi rh$Area of the Base $= \pi r^2$Total Painted Area $= 2\pi rh + \pi r^2$3. Substitute the ValuesGiven:$r = 28 \text{ cm}$$h = 80 \text{ cm}$$\pi = \frac{22}{7}$Part A: Curved Surface Area$$CSA = 2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 28 \times 80$$$$CSA = 2 \times 22 \times 4 \times 80$$(since $28 \div 7 = 4$)$$CSA = 14,080 \text{ cm}^2$$Part B: Base Area$$\text{Base Area} = \frac{22}{7} \times 28 \times 28$$$$\text{Base Area} = 22 \times 4 \times 28$$(since $28 \div 7 = 4$)$$\text{Base Area} = 2,464 \text{ cm}^2$$4. Calculate Total Area$$\text{Total Area} = 14,080 + 2,464$$$$\text{Total Area} = 16,544 \text{ cm}^2$$Final Answer:The total area of the surfaces painted is 16,544 cm².

fields.question_text: The figure below is a structure in Marsabit. Its external surface is to be painted. Calculate the total area of the surface to be painted.
fields.explanation: Mathematical BreakdownTo find the total surface area, we calculate the sum of the two visible parts:Curved Surface Area (CSA) of the Cylinder: The vertical "walls" of the tank.Surface Area of the Hemispherical Lid: The dome covering the top.2. Calculation StepsStep A: Cylindrical Part (Walls)The formula for the curved surface area is $2\pi rh$.$$CSA = 2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 7 \times 10$$Simplify by canceling the 7s ($7 \div 7 = 1$).$2 \times 22 \times 10 = 440$Area of Cylinder = $440\text{ m}^2$Step B: Hemispherical Lid (Dome)A full sphere is $4\pi r^2$, so a half-sphere is $2\pi r^2$.$$\text{Area} = 2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 7 \times 7$$Simplify by canceling one 7 ($7 \div 7 = 1$).$2 \times 22 \times 7 = 308$Area of Lid = $308\text{ m}^2$Step C: Total Surface Area$$\text{Total Area} = \text{Cylinder Area} + \text{Lid Area}$$$$\text{Total Area} = 440\text{ m}^2 + 308\text{ m}^2 = 748\text{ m}^2$$3. Final AnswerThe total surface area of the tank is $748\text{ m}^2$.

fields.question_text:
A community in Marsabit constructed a water tank consisting of a cylindrical body with a hemispherical lid on top.
If this tank was to be painted and one tin of paint covers $20\text{ m}^2$, how many full tins of paint would the community need to buy?
fields.explanation:
1. Mathematical BreakdownTo find the total surface area, we calculate the sum of the two visible parts:Curved Surface Area (CSA) of the Cylinder: The vertical "walls" of the tank.Surface Area of the Hemispherical Lid: The dome covering the top.2. Calculation StepsStep A: Cylindrical Part (Walls)The formula for the curved surface area is $2\pi rh$.$$CSA = 2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 7 \times 10$$Simplify by canceling the 7s ($7 \div 7 = 1$).$2 \times 22 \times 10 = 440$Area of Cylinder = $440\text{ m}^2$Step B: Hemispherical Lid (Dome)A full sphere is $4\pi r^2$, so a half-sphere is $2\pi r^2$.$$\text{Area} = 2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 7 \times 7$$Simplify by canceling one 7 ($7 \div 7 = 1$).$2 \times 22 \times 7 = 308$Area of Lid = $308\text{ m}^2$Step C: Total Surface Area$$\text{Total Area} = \text{Cylinder Area} + \text{Lid Area}$$$$\text{Total Area} = 440\text{ m}^2 + 308\text{ m}^2 = 748\text{ m}^2$$3. Final AnswerThe total surface area of the tank is $748\text{ m}^2$.
$$\text{Number of tins} = \frac{748}{20} = 37.4$$Since you cannot buy $0.4$ of a tin, the community must buy 38 full tins.
